🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.175%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship between inverse trigonometric functions and their derivatives. Re-teach the fundamental formulas and their applications.
Q21.125%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the basic approach but make errors in applying trigonometric identities during simplification. Practice step-by-step identity applications.
Q21.2100%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand parametric and implicit differentiation techniques. Re-teach the fundamental concepts and provide step-by-step examples.
Q2240%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of cross (vector) product. Re-teach the fundamental definition and properties of vector cross product.
Q2240%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the cross product method but make errors in applying the procedure correctly. Drill the step-by-step calculation process.
Q2220%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method and steps but make arithmetic errors in cross product calculations. Practice computational accuracy.
Q2340%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the inverse trig concepts but make errors in applying the correct sequence of algebraic operations. Drill the step-by-step methodology.
Q2320%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the algebraic properties of inverse trigonometric functions. Re-teach the fundamental identities and domain-range relationships.
Q2320%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the question requirements for inverse trigonometric algebra. Clarify what algebraic manipulation is expected.
Q2320%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in inverse trig calculations. Practice computational accuracy with inverse functions.
Q2471%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the continuity concept but make errors in applying the three conditions or in algebraic manipulation. Review the step-by-step process for checking continuity.
Q2429%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental concept of continuity or its three conditions. Re-teach the definition and conditions for continuity at a point.
Q25.183%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand integration by substitution method. Re-teach the fundamental steps of u-substitution and when to apply it.
Q25.117%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the substitution method but make procedural errors in execution. Practice step-by-step application with guided examples.
Q25.250%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental concept being tested in this question. Re-teach the core method step-by-step.
Q25.250%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in executing the steps correctly. Drill the procedure with guided practice.
Q2667%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand conditional probability concepts. Re-teach Bayes' theorem and conditional probability formulas.
Q2617%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the conditional probability method but make procedural errors in applying formulas. Practice step-by-step problem solving.
Q2616%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the concept and steps but make arithmetic errors in probability calculations. Practice fraction computations.
Q2788%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differentiation method for implicit functions but make errors in applying chain rule or handling composite functions. Practice step-by-step differentiation with guided examples.
Q2712%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstood the question and attempted unrelated problems. Emphasize careful reading and identifying what needs to be differentiated.
Q28.150%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for differentiating exponential and logarithmic functions but make execution errors in the steps. Review the differentiation rules and provide more practice problems.
Q28.150%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental concepts of differentiating exponential and logarithmic functions. Re-teach the basic differentiation rules for these function types from first principles.
Q28.283%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand parametric and implicit differentiation techniques. Re-teach the fundamental methods for differentiating parametric equations.
Q28.217%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differentiation method but make errors in applying the steps correctly. Practice step-by-step execution of parametric differentiation.
Q29.150%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for finding equations of lines but make execution errors in the steps. Review step-by-step procedures and common algebraic manipulations.
Q29.150%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental concepts of equation of a line in 3D geometry. Re-teach the basic principles and different forms of line equations.
Q29.250%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for finding equations of lines but make execution errors in the steps. Review step-by-step procedures and common algebraic manipulations.
Q29.250%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental concepts of equation of a line in 3D geometry. Re-teach the basic principles and different forms of line equations.
Q3057%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up integrals for finding area under curves. Re-teach the fundamental concept of representing area as definite integrals.
Q3043%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in setting up limits or evaluating the integral. Drill the step-by-step process for area calculations.
Q3150%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the graphical method for linear programming. Re-teach the fundamental steps of plotting constraints and finding feasible regions.
Q3150%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in executing the steps. Drill the sequence: plot constraints, identify feasible region, find corner points, evaluate objective function.
Q3267%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental concepts of angle between lines in 3D geometry. Re-teach the method step-by-step with clear examples.
Q3233%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the concept but make arithmetic errors during calculations. Practice computational skills and verify each calculation step.
Q3360%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to find adjoint and inverse of matrices. Re-teach the fundamental concepts and calculation methods.
Q3340%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in the sequential steps for finding adjoint and inverse. Drill the step-by-step procedure.
Q34.167%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the differential equation application context. Review how to identify growth/decay scenarios and set up the appropriate differential equation.
Q34.133%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental principles of growth/decay differential equations. Re-teach the exponential model and separation of variables method.

Q34.233%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental principles of growth/decay differential equations. Re-teach the exponential model and separation of variables method.

Q35.133%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know integration rules but make errors in applying them systematically. Drill the step-by-step procedure with practice problems.

🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.160%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to differentiate inverse trigonometric functions with composite expressions. Re-teach the chain rule for inverse trig functions.
Q21.140%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differentiation rules but make errors in applying quotient rule or simplifying algebraic expressions. Practice step-by-step differentiation.
Q21.278%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the parametric differentiation method but make errors in applying the chain rule for second derivatives. Review the formula d²y/dx² = (d/dθ(dy/dx))/(dx/dθ) step-by-step.
Q21.213%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the correct formula for second derivative of parametric equations. Re-teach that d²y/dx² ≠ d²y/dθ² ÷ dx/dθ, but requires the chain rule application.
Q21.29%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method and steps correct but make arithmetic errors when evaluating trigonometric values at θ = π/6. Practice calculating sec²(π/6) and cos⁴(π/6) accurately.
Q2275%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to prove vector parallelism using cross products. Re-teach that vectors are parallel when their cross product equals zero.
Q2225%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the approach but make algebraic errors in expanding cross products. Practice the distributive property of cross products systematically.
Q23100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply trigonometric identities correctly for inverse trig simplification. Re-teach the compound angle formula and properties of inverse trigonometric functions.
Q2450%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the continuity method but make errors in the execution. They get confused with the function value at x=0 or make sign errors. Drill the step-by-step process of checking continuity.
Q2438%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of continuity at a point or how to find limits of piecewise functions. Re-teach the definition of continuity and the LHL = RHL = f(a) condition.
Q2412%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors when simplifying the limit. Practice more limit calculations and algebraic manipulation.
Q25.163%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand integration by substitution for logarithmic functions. Re-teach substitution methods with log x = t transformation.
Q25.137%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the substitution method but make errors in applying the integration by parts formula. Practice step-by-step execution of the method.
Q25.261%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to split the integral at x = -2/3 but make errors in setting up the absolute value or limits. Review proper handling of areas below the x-axis.
Q25.223%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand that area requires absolute values when the function goes below the x-axis. Re-teach the concept of |f(x)| for area calculations.
Q25.28%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the integral correctly but make arithmetic errors in evaluation. Practice integration computations with fractions.
Q25.28%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand which limits to use for the area calculation. Emphasize careful reading of bounded region specifications.
Q2644%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method to find probability of at least 2 hits but make errors in setting up cases or applying inclusion-exclusion principle. Review the systematic approach for 'at least' problems.
Q2644%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to approach 'at least 2' probability problems or confuse independent events with mutually exclusive events. Re-teach the fundamental approach to compound probability.
Q2612%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in fraction calculations or LCM operations. Practice fraction arithmetic and common denominator calculations.
Q27100%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship between median and side in an equilateral triangle. Re-teach the geometric properties of equilateral triangles and how median equals altitude.
Q28.175%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the logarithmic differentiation method but make algebraic errors in simplification steps. Review fraction operations and equation rearrangement.
Q28.113%Read Questions WrongStudents used substitution method instead of implicit differentiation. Emphasize reading the question carefully to identify the required approach.
Q28.112%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand implicit differentiation with logarithms. Re-teach logarithmic differentiation fundamentals.
Q28.2100%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differentiation method but make errors in algebraic manipulation or don't complete the simplification to reach the required form. Practice the complete solution steps until the final answer.
Q29.183%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for finding distance from point to line but make errors in cross product calculation or dot product steps. Practice vector operations and check intermediate steps.
Q29.117%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to find distance from point to line in 3D. Re-teach the perpendicular distance formula and its derivation.
Q29.283%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for finding distance from point to line but make errors in cross product calculation or dot product steps. Practice vector operations and check intermediate steps.
Q29.217%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to find distance from point to line in 3D. Re-teach the perpendicular distance formula and its derivation.
Q3066%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the integration method but make errors in bounds, setup, or final calculation steps. Review proper integration bounds and double-check calculation procedures.
Q3034%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value functions in area calculations or how to set up the integration properly. Re-teach absolute value integration and area calculation methods.
Q3183%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the linear programming method but make errors in identifying correct corner points or fail to check unbounded region properly. Practice the complete unbounded region verification process.
Q3117%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of unbounded regions in linear programming. Re-teach when and how to check for minimum values in unbounded feasible regions.
Q3268%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know how to set up the angle condition and parametric equations but make algebraic errors when simplifying. Review algebraic manipulation of quadratic equations.
Q3232%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to find lines through origin at fixed angles to given lines. Re-teach the dot product formula for angles between lines.
Q3350%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ but make errors in matrix multiplication or transpose steps. Practice step-by-step matrix operations.
Q3329%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the reversal law (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ or confuse it with other formulas. Re-teach the inverse property clearly.
Q3321%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in matrix multiplication. Focus on careful calculation of matrix products.
Q34.157%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to identify or set up differential equations for growth/decay applications. Re-teach the fundamental principles of exponential growth models.
Q34.129%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differential equation method but make errors in the solution steps or integration process. Practice step-by-step solving techniques.
Q34.114%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method and steps but make arithmetic errors in calculations. Focus on computational accuracy in logarithmic and exponential expressions.
Q34.257%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to identify or set up differential equations for growth/decay applications. Re-teach the fundamental principles of exponential growth models.
Q34.229%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differential equation method but make errors in the solution steps or integration process. Practice step-by-step solving techniques.
Q34.214%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method and steps but make arithmetic errors in calculations. Focus on computational accuracy in logarithmic and exponential expressions.
Q35.150%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know partial fractions method but make errors in setting up the decomposition or solving for constants. Practice coefficient comparison and substitution methods.
Q35.138%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to factorize the denominator or apply partial fractions correctly. Re-teach factorization of cubic expressions and partial fractions decomposition.
Q35.112%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in calculating constants or final integration. Practice careful calculation checking.
Q35.256%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the King's property and substitution method but make errors in limit changes or final evaluation. Practice limit transformations and careful computation of inverse trigonometric values.
Q35.233%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply King's property for definite integrals or the standard substitution techniques. Re-teach the property ∫₀ᵃ f(x)dx = ∫₀ᵃ f(a-x)dx and integration by substitution.
Q35.211%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in the final evaluation of tan⁻¹(∞). Practice evaluating limits of inverse trigonometric functions.
Q3640%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what parts (ii) and (iii) are asking for, solving for wrong probabilities. Clarify the difference between conditional probability questions and joint probability calculations.
Q3640%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the Bayes' theorem setup right but make arithmetic errors in fraction calculations and decimal conversions. Practice fraction arithmetic and common denominator calculations.
Q3610%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the basic structure of Bayes' theorem problems with multiple machines. Re-teach conditional probability and total probability theorem.
Q3610%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know Bayes' theorem but confuse P(D|B) with P(B|D) in their setup. Drill the correct formula application and what each term represents.
Q3758%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for finding maximum area but make errors in derivative calculation or simplification steps. Review product rule and chain rule applications carefully.
Q3718%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the problem by finding area of ellipse instead of inscribed rectangle or using wrong formulation. Clarify that the rectangle is inscribed in the ellipse.
Q3712%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method and steps right but make arithmetic errors in simplification or substitution. Practice algebraic manipulation with square roots.
Q3712%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up the area function for inscribed rectangle or miss the constraint equation. Re-teach optimization problems with constraints.
Q3850%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the formal definitions of injective and surjective functions. Re-teach one-to-one and onto concepts with clear examples.
Q3833%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in discriminant calculations or factoring. Practice quadratic solving techniques.
Q3817%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand what needs to be proven for injective/surjective properties. Clarify question requirements and proof structure.

🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.180%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to differentiate inverse trigonometric composite functions. Re-teach chain rule application for inverse trig functions step-by-step.
Q21.120%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differentiation formula but make errors in applying chain rule or quotient rule. Drill the sequence of steps for composite function differentiation.
Q21.270%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the parametric differentiation method but make errors in applying the chain rule or simplifying expressions. Drill the step-by-step process for second derivatives.
Q21.217%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the formula for second derivative of parametric equations. Re-teach the concept that d²y/dx² = (d/dθ(dy/dx))/(dx/dθ).
Q21.213%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors when substituting θ = π/6 or computing trigonometric values. Practice evaluating trig functions at standard angles.
Q2245%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to use cross products to prove vector parallelism. Re-teach the fundamental principle that vectors are parallel when their cross product equals zero.
Q2233%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in expanding cross products or applying vector algebra properties. Drill the systematic expansion of (a-d) × (b-c).
Q2211%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the approach right but make arithmetic errors in cross product calculations. Practice cross product computations and sign handling.
Q2211%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand what needs to be proven or confuse the given conditions. Emphasize careful reading of vector proof problems.
Q2358%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use trigonometric identities to simplify inverse trig expressions. Re-teach the sine addition formula and its application with 1/√2 = cos(π/4) = sin(π/4).
Q2314%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but miss the final step of applying sin⁻¹(sin(θ)) = θ. Emphasize completing the simplification process.
Q2314%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the trigonometric identity right but make errors in the angle addition or final simplification. Practice careful arithmetic with π/4 operations.
Q2314%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand what needs to be simplified or make incorrect assumptions about the expression. Clarify that the entire expression needs to be simplified to its simplest form.
Q2438%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand continuity conditions for piecewise functions. Re-teach that LHL = RHL = f(0) at the point of discontinuity.
Q2431%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the continuity concept but make errors in limit evaluation or algebraic manipulation. Practice rationalization and limit computation steps.
Q2419%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the parameter as 'b' instead of 'p' or misinterpret the domain intervals. Emphasize careful reading of function definitions.
Q2412%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in final calculations. Practice basic algebraic operations and fraction simplification.
Q25.163%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to handle integrals with logarithmic functions in both numerator and denominator. Re-teach substitution techniques for logarithmic integrals.
Q25.125%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know substitution method but make errors in algebraic manipulation after substituting t = 1 + log x. Practice step-by-step algebraic simplification.
Q25.112%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the substitution right but make arithmetic errors in the final integration steps. Practice basic integration formulas.
Q25.255%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to split the integral at x = -2/3 where line crosses x-axis but make errors in setting up absolute values or computation steps. Drill the systematic approach to area problems involving sign changes.
Q25.222%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the problem correctly but make arithmetic errors when evaluating definite integrals or combining fractions. Practice computational accuracy with fraction operations.
Q25.217%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand that area requires absolute value when function goes below x-axis. Re-teach the concept of area vs definite integral and when to use |f(x)|.
Q25.26%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstood what region to find area for. Emphasize careful reading of area problems and identifying correct boundaries.
Q2660%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of 'at least 2' meaning exactly 2 OR all 3 hitting the target. They miss cases or use wrong probability formulas. Re-teach the definition of 'at least' and enumerate all favorable outcomes.
Q2620%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret 'at least 2 hit target' as complement of 'at most 1' but set up the complement incorrectly. Clarify the relationship between 'at least' and 'at most' terminology.
Q2615%Make Calculation MistakesStudents identify the correct cases but make arithmetic errors in fraction calculations or LCM computation. Practice fraction operations and encourage verification of calculations.
Q265%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but forget to include the case where all three hit the target. Emphasize checking all possible favorable outcomes for 'at least' problems.
Q2755%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship between median and side in an equilateral triangle. Re-teach that median = (√3/2) × side using Pythagorean theorem.
Q2736%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the median formula but make errors in differentiation or algebraic manipulation. Drill chain rule and fraction operations.
Q279%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what quantity is changing or what needs to be found. Emphasize careful reading of rate of change problems.
Q28.162%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the logarithmic differentiation method but make algebraic errors while rearranging terms or isolating dy/dx. Emphasize careful algebraic manipulation and checking intermediate steps.
Q28.138%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply logarithmic differentiation to implicit functions. Re-teach the systematic approach: take log, differentiate both sides, solve for dy/dx.
Q28.257%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply implicit differentiation to parametric equations. Re-teach implicit differentiation rules and parametric differentiation step-by-step.
Q28.243%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying the chain rule or product rule correctly. Practice step-by-step differentiation of composite functions.
Q29.150%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the correct formula for perpendicular distance from point to line in 3D. Re-teach the formula and method step-by-step.
Q29.150%Read Questions WrongStudents solved for different problems instead of finding perpendicular distance from given point to line. Clarify the question requirements.
Q29.246%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the formula and method but make arithmetic errors in cross products, dot products, or final calculations. Practice vector calculations step-by-step.
Q29.236%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the formula for shortest distance between two skew lines. Re-teach the formula: d = |(a₂-a₁)·(b₁×b₂)|/|b₁×b₂|.
Q29.218%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula but mix up direction vectors or position vectors. Emphasize careful identification of a₁, a₂, b₁, b₂ from line equations.
Q3052%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in setting up limits or forgetting the factor of 2. Drill the steps for absolute value integration.
Q3026%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value in integration or misinterpret the curve equation. Re-teach absolute value graphing and integration setup.
Q3013%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up correctly but make arithmetic errors in integration or limits evaluation. Practice integration calculations.
Q309%Read Questions WrongStudents solve different problems or misread the curve equation. Emphasize careful reading of integration problems.
Q3162%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the linear programming method but make errors in finding corner points or evaluating the objective function. Review systematic approach to corner point identification.
Q3115%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up feasible regions or handle unbounded regions. Re-teach graphical method fundamentals.
Q3115%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret constraints or objective function setup. Emphasize careful reading of linear programming problems.
Q318%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in computing objective function values. Practice numerical calculations.
Q3252%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to find lines through origin at fixed angles to given lines. Re-teach the formula for angle between lines using direction ratios.
Q3248%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the angle formula but make errors in setting up equations or solving the quadratic. Practice step-by-step problem solving.
Q3360%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ but make errors in cofactor calculations or matrix multiplication steps. Drill the systematic calculation of cofactors and matrix multiplication.
Q3320%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the inverse formula (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ or matrix inversion methods. Re-teach the property of inverse of product and adjugate method.
Q3315%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in determinant calculation or matrix multiplication. Practice computational accuracy with sign errors.
Q335%Read Questions WrongStudents solve for B instead of (AB)⁻¹ or compute AB first then find inverse. Emphasize using the direct property (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹.
Q34.150%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up differential equations for growth/decay problems. Re-teach the fundamental concept that rate of change is proportional to current quantity.
Q34.150%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the differential equation setup right but make arithmetic errors when calculating the constant or final time. Practice logarithm calculations and substitution steps.
Q34.243%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the differential equation structure and make errors in applying the product rule. Clarify the correct form of linear differential equations.
Q34.243%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the method for solving linear differential equations. Re-teach the integrating factor technique step-by-step.
Q34.214%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in the integration steps. Practice integration by parts with polynomial and trigonometric functions.
Q35.152%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the partial fraction method but make errors in factorization, coefficient calculation, or integration steps. Practice systematic coefficient substitution and verification.
Q35.148%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand partial fraction decomposition for rational functions. Re-teach the systematic approach for proper factorization and coefficient determination.
Q35.292%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply properties of definite integrals like King's property for this type of problem. Re-teach the standard approach of using ∫₀ᵃ f(x)dx = ∫₀ᵃ f(a-x)dx.
Q35.28%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the property and method but make errors in limit evaluation or substitution steps. Practice the complete solution methodology.
Q3654%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the proper application of total probability theorem and Bayes' theorem in conditional probability problems. Re-teach these fundamental probability concepts with step-by-step examples.
Q3621%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what probability is being asked for in each part, especially confusing conditional probability with joint probability. Clarify the difference between P(A|B) and P(A∩B).
Q3613%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the probability formulas but make errors in applying Bayes' theorem correctly, especially in the denominator calculation. Practice step-by-step Bayes' theorem problems.
Q3612%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors when multiplying fractions and finding common denominators. Practice fraction calculations and decimal conversions.
Q3754%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to set up the area function for a rectangle inscribed in an ellipse. Re-teach the method of expressing area as A = 4xy using ellipse equation to substitute for y.
Q3723%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in differentiating the area function or applying the product rule. Drill the differentiation steps systematically.
Q3715%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method and steps right but mess up arithmetic in solving a² - 2x² = 0. Practice algebraic manipulation and simplification.
Q378%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the problem as finding area using integration instead of optimization. Clarify that this is a maximization problem using derivatives.
Q3867%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of injective vs surjective functions or the properties of equivalence relations. Re-teach the fundamental definitions and methods for checking these properties.
Q3833%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the approach but make errors in the logical flow or skip crucial steps in proving injectivity/surjectivity. Practice step-by-step proof writing.

🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.168%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the derivative formula for cos⁻¹(x) or how to apply chain rule with composite functions. Re-teach the derivative formulas for inverse trigonometric functions step-by-step.
Q21.132%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the derivative formula but make errors in applying quotient rule or simplifying complex expressions. Practice chain rule with quotient rule systematically.
Q21.263%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the parametric differentiation method but make errors in applying the chain rule or computing d²y/dx². Practice the formula d²y/dx² = (d/dθ(dy/dx))/(dx/dθ).
Q21.221%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of second-order parametric differentiation. Re-teach the relationship between parametric equations and second derivatives.
Q21.211%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the parametric equations or confuse the required derivative. Emphasize careful reading of parametric forms and what derivative is being asked.
Q21.25%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in trigonometric calculations. Practice computing trigonometric values at specific angles.
Q2270%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the approach to prove vector parallelism using cross product. Re-teach that two vectors are parallel if their cross product equals zero.
Q2226%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in expanding the cross product or substituting given conditions. Practice step-by-step expansion of (a⃗ - d⃗) × (b⃗ - c⃗).
Q224%Read Questions WrongStudents confused dot product with cross product for parallel vectors. Clarify that parallel vectors have cross product = 0, not dot product = 0.
Q2367%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use trigonometric identities to simplify inverse trig expressions. Re-teach sin(A+B) = sinA cosB + cosA sinB and standard angle values.
Q2325%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying trigonometric identities or formulas. Practice step-by-step application of sin(A±B) formulas.
Q238%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in simplification. Review basic trigonometric calculations and standard angle values.
Q2452%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to check continuity by equating left-hand and right-hand limits. Re-teach the fundamental concept of continuity.
Q2428%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying rationalization or L'Hôpital's rule. Drill the correct steps.
Q2420%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but mess up arithmetic in final calculations. Practice computational accuracy.
Q25.146%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know integration by substitution but make errors in transforming the integrand or handling the differential. Review the substitution process step-by-step.
Q25.139%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand integration by substitution technique. Re-teach the method with emphasis on proper substitution and differential handling.
Q25.115%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in integration or evaluation. Practice computational accuracy.
Q25.282%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the concept of area under curves but make errors in setting up limits or forget to split integrals at x-intercept. Practice identifying where to split the region.
Q25.29%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the problem correctly but make arithmetic errors in evaluating definite integrals. Review calculation of fractions and substitution.
Q25.29%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of area under curves or when to use absolute values. Re-teach the fundamental approach to area problems.
Q2670%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to handle 'at least 2' probability problems. Re-teach the concept that 'at least 2' means exactly 2 OR exactly 3, requiring separate calculation of each case.
Q2620%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in identifying the correct cases or calculating complements properly. Practice identifying all possible combinations for 'at least 2' scenarios.
Q2610%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand what 'at least 2 people hit the target' means. Clarify that this requires identifying exactly which combinations satisfy this condition.
Q2755%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship between median and side of equilateral triangle. Re-teach geometric relationships and properties of equilateral triangles.
Q2727%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the geometric relationships but make errors in differentiation steps. Practice chain rule and related rates systematically.
Q2718%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in simplifying expressions. Practice algebraic manipulation with radicals.
Q28.167%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use logarithmic differentiation properly for implicit functions. Re-teach the logarithmic differentiation method step-by-step.
Q28.133%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the logarithmic method but make errors in differentiation steps or algebraic manipulation. Drill the differentiation rules and algebraic simplification.
Q28.267%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know how to differentiate implicitly but make errors in algebraic manipulation and factoring. Drill the algebraic steps after differentiation.
Q28.233%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to differentiate implicit functions involving composite functions. Re-teach implicit differentiation with chain rule.
Q29.167%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the formula for shortest distance between two skew lines in 3D. Re-teach the vector method step-by-step.
Q29.133%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula but make errors in cross product calculation or dot product computation. Practice vector operations systematically.
Q29.267%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the formula for shortest distance between two skew lines in 3D. Re-teach the vector method step-by-step.
Q29.233%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula but make errors in cross product calculation or dot product computation. Practice vector operations systematically.
Q3050%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know how to find area using integration but make errors in setting up limits or evaluating integrals. Practice proper setup of integration bounds and careful evaluation.
Q3030%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value in geometric curves or set up area integrals properly. Re-teach absolute value graphing and area calculation methods.
Q3020%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the setup right but make arithmetic errors in integration or final calculations. Practice careful computation and verification of results.
Q3152%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in finding corner points or evaluating the objective function. Practice intersection calculations and systematic evaluation at all vertices.
Q3124%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the graphical method or how to handle unbounded regions. Re-teach the complete procedure including feasibility region identification.
Q3114%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors when computing objective function values. Practice fraction arithmetic and substitution.
Q3110%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the objective function or constraints. Emphasize careful reading and checking of signs in linear programming problems.
Q3268%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply the angle between lines formula for lines passing through origin. Re-teach the method step-by-step.
Q3220%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but mess up algebraic manipulations and squaring operations. Practice computational accuracy in vector calculations.
Q3212%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula but make errors in setting up the direction ratios correctly. Practice identifying direction ratios from line equations.
Q3341%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the inverse property (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹. Re-teach the fundamental property and why order matters in matrix multiplication.
Q3335%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in cofactor calculations or matrix multiplication. Practice basic matrix operations.
Q3324%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the property but skip steps in finding adjoint or make errors in transpose operations. Emphasize systematic step-by-step approach.
Q34.150%Make Calculation MistakesStudents correctly identify this as a linear differential equation but make arithmetic errors in integration by parts. Practice integration by parts with polynomial and trigonometric functions.
Q34.133%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for solving linear differential equations but make errors in applying the integrating factor formula or miss steps in the solution process. Drill the standard procedure step-by-step.
Q34.117%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the differential equation structure and attempt incorrect solution methods. Clarify how to identify and set up linear differential equations properly.
Q34.250%Make Calculation MistakesStudents correctly identify this as a linear differential equation but make arithmetic errors in integration by parts. Practice integration by parts with polynomial and trigonometric functions.
Q34.233%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for solving linear differential equations but make errors in applying the integrating factor formula or miss steps in the solution process. Drill the standard procedure step-by-step.
Q34.217%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the differential equation structure and attempt incorrect solution methods. Clarify how to identify and set up linear differential equations properly.
Q35.156%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use partial fraction decomposition for rational functions. Re-teach the complete method with proper factorization.
Q35.138%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know partial fractions but make errors in setting up equations or solving for constants. Drill the step-by-step process.
Q35.16%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in coefficient calculations. Practice algebraic manipulation.
Q35.250%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply the King's rule property for definite integrals. Re-teach the fundamental property ∫₀ᵃ f(x)dx = ∫₀ᵃ f(a-x)dx step-by-step.
Q35.244%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to use King's rule but make errors in algebraic manipulation or trigonometric substitution steps. Drill the complete solution process with careful attention to substitution limits.
Q35.26%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstood the problem requirements. Clarify what the question is asking and emphasize careful reading of the integral bounds and expression.
Q3644%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpreted what each part was asking for and calculated wrong probabilities. Clarify what conditional probability, Bayes' theorem, and 'produced by B or C' mean in context.
Q3628%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the formulas but make arithmetic errors in fraction calculations and decimal conversions. Practice fraction operations and check calculations step-by-step.
Q3621%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply Bayes' theorem and conditional probability formulas correctly. Re-teach the conceptual framework and when to use which formula.
Q367%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the concepts but miss steps in the Bayes' theorem application. Drill the complete step-by-step procedure for conditional probability problems.
Q3757%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the ellipse constraint or how to set up the optimization problem. Re-teach how to substitute ellipse equation into area formula.
Q3729%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in derivative calculation or critical point identification. Practice product rule and chain rule applications.
Q3714%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the setup right but make arithmetic errors in simplifying expressions. Practice algebraic manipulation.
Q3850%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know how to check injectivity and surjectivity but make errors in the algebraic steps or logical reasoning. Review the step-by-step process for proving function properties.
Q3833%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the definitions of injective and surjective functions or equivalence relations. Re-teach the fundamental concepts with examples.
Q3817%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the concepts but make arithmetic errors in solving equations or factoring. Practice algebraic manipulation skills.

🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.164%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to differentiate inverse trigonometric functions or apply substitution methods. Re-teach derivative formulas for inverse trig functions and substitution techniques.
Q21.136%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying chain rule or substitution steps. Practice step-by-step differentiation with careful attention to intermediate steps.
Q21.264%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the parametric differentiation method but make errors in applying the chain rule for second derivatives. Review the formula d²y/dx² = (d/dθ)(dy/dx) × (dθ/dx).
Q21.228%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to find second derivatives of parametric equations. Re-teach the complete method from dy/dx to d²y/dx².
Q21.28%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method and steps right but make arithmetic errors in computing trigonometric values at θ = π/6. Practice substituting specific angle values.
Q2256%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the cross product expansion method but make algebraic errors or incorrect substitutions. Drill the step-by-step expansion of (a⃗ - d⃗) × (b⃗ - c⃗) and careful substitution of given conditions.
Q2244%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to use cross product properties to prove vector parallelism. Teach the fundamental principle that if cross product equals zero, vectors are parallel.
Q2364%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use trigonometric identities to simplify expressions inside inverse sine. Re-teach sin(A+B) identity and its application to inverse trigonometric functions.
Q2336%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the trigonometric identities but make errors in applying them correctly or miss intermediate steps. Practice step-by-step application of sin(A+B) formula.
Q2456%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the continuity method but make errors in setting up limits or algebraic manipulation. Review step-by-step limit evaluation and rationalization techniques.
Q2433%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand continuity conditions or how to set up LHL = RHL at discontinuous points. Re-teach continuity definition and limit evaluation.
Q2411%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in rationalization or limit evaluation. Practice algebraic manipulation and substitution.
Q25.1100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply integration by substitution. Re-teach the method with proper substitution techniques and practice identifying when to use this method.
Q25.243%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to split integration at x = -2/3 but make errors in setting up limits or applying integration rules. Practice setting up proper limits for area calculations.
Q25.229%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand that area below x-axis needs absolute value or proper limit splitting. Re-teach concept of signed area vs geometric area.
Q25.224%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up integration correctly but make arithmetic errors in substituting limits or simplifying fractions. Practice fraction arithmetic and limit substitution.
Q25.24%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the integration limits or area boundaries. Emphasize careful reading of given constraints.
Q2642%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in calculating individual probability terms or miss certain cases. Review the systematic approach for identifying all possible scenarios.
Q2637%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to identify all cases for 'at least 2' or incorrectly calculate joint probabilities. Re-teach the concept of mutually exclusive events and independent probabilities.
Q2621%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors when adding fractions or calculating products. Practice fraction arithmetic and emphasize careful calculation.
Q2763%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the relationship between median and side but make errors in the differentiation steps or algebraic manipulation. Review chain rule and implicit differentiation techniques.
Q2737%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the correct formula for median of equilateral triangle or confuse it with other geometric properties. Re-teach that median = (√3/2)×side for equilateral triangles.
Q28.146%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to take logarithms but make errors in differentiation steps or simplification. Drill the systematic approach to implicit differentiation.
Q28.140%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the logarithmic differentiation method for implicit functions. Re-teach taking log of both sides and differentiating using chain rule.
Q28.17%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make algebraic errors in simplification. Practice algebraic manipulation with fractions.
Q28.17%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the given equation or attempted wrong differentiation approach. Emphasize careful reading of implicit function problems.
Q28.255%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know implicit differentiation but make errors in applying product rule or chain rule. Practice step-by-step differentiation of composite functions.
Q28.227%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand implicit differentiation methods. Re-teach the fundamental approach to differentiating equations implicitly.
Q28.218%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method but make sign errors or algebraic manipulation mistakes. Practice trigonometric simplifications.
Q29.160%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the perpendicular distance formula for point to line in 3D. Re-teach the method using dot product of perpendicular vector.
Q29.120%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstood the problem and tried to find distance between two lines instead of point to line distance. Emphasize reading the question carefully.
Q29.120%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the method but make arithmetic errors in cross product calculations or final distance computation. Practice computational skills.
Q29.250%Read Questions WrongStudents compute shortest distance between skew lines correctly but misinterpret what the question is asking for. Review question requirements and ensure students understand what needs to be found.
Q29.225%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the formula and method for shortest distance between skew lines but make arithmetic errors in cross product calculations. Practice vector arithmetic and cross product computations.
Q29.225%Foundational GapsStudents don't properly understand the formula for shortest distance between skew lines. Re-teach the concept and derivation of the shortest distance formula.
Q3054%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know how to set up the area integral but make errors in limits, factor of 2, or integration steps. Drill the complete method with emphasis on symmetry and proper limits.
Q3029%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value in area problems or how to set up the integral for bounded regions. Re-teach area under curves with absolute value functions.
Q3013%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the problem correctly but make arithmetic errors in integration or final calculation. Practice basic integration and fraction arithmetic.
Q304%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the equation or boundaries of the region. Emphasize careful reading of the curve equation and boundary conditions.
Q3169%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in identifying the feasible region or corner points. Practice systematic approach to finding intersection points and checking constraints.
Q3121%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors when calculating objective function values at corner points. Drill computation of Z = -3x - 5y at given coordinates.
Q315%Read Questions WrongStudents misread constraints or objective function. Emphasize careful reading of linear programming problems and double-checking constraint inequalities.
Q315%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of unbounded regions in linear programming. Re-teach when minimum/maximum values exist vs when they don't for unbounded feasible regions.
Q3275%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to find equations of lines passing through origin at a given angle to another line. Re-teach the concept of angle between lines using dot product formula.
Q3225%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the angle formula but make errors in setting up the system of equations or solving them. Practice step-by-step problem solving for finding lines at specific angles.
Q3342%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in matrix multiplication or adjoint calculation. Practice computational accuracy in matrix operations.
Q3333%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula but confuse order of multiplication or skip steps in adjoint calculation. Emphasize step-by-step procedure.
Q3325%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the property (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ or struggle with inverse matrix concepts. Re-teach inverse matrix properties and methods.
Q34.150%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know how to set up the differential equation dN/dt = kN but make errors in solving or applying boundary conditions. Review the complete solution process step-by-step.
Q34.138%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to model bacterial growth using differential equations. Re-teach the connection between rate of change and exponential growth.
Q34.112%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the problem correctly but make arithmetic errors in logarithmic calculations. Practice logarithm properties and exponential calculations.
Q34.275%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the linear differential equation method but make errors in applying the integrating factor or setting up the equation correctly. Drill the step-by-step procedure for linear DEs.
Q34.225%Foundational GapsStudents don't recognize this as a linear differential equation or don't know the standard solution method. Re-teach the identification and solution of linear DEs.
Q35.151%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply partial fractions for rational functions with repeated linear factors. Re-teach partial fraction decomposition with proper form A/(x-1) + B/(x-1)² + C/(x+1).
Q35.133%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know partial fractions but make errors in setting up equations or solving for coefficients. Practice systematic coefficient comparison and substitution methods.
Q35.18%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in coefficient calculations. Focus on careful algebraic manipulation and checking work.
Q35.18%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstood the problem requirements or setup. Emphasize careful reading of integral expressions and proper factorization.
Q35.250%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the property method but make errors in substitution or integration steps. Review the standard integral formula and substitution technique.
Q35.242%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply the definite integral property ∫₀^π f(x)dx = ∫₀^π f(π-x)dx. Re-teach this fundamental property with examples.
Q35.28%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in the final computation. Practice calculation of inverse trigonometric limits.
Q3655%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand when to apply Bayes' theorem vs basic probability rules. Re-teach the conceptual difference between P(A|B) and P(A∩B).
Q3622%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formulas but apply them in wrong sequence or miss denominators in conditional probability. Drill the step-by-step process.
Q3617%Read Questions WrongStudents misread what probability is being asked for - confusing conditional with joint probability. Practice careful question interpretation.
Q366%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in fraction calculations. Practice fraction operations.
Q3750%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in the differentiation steps or critical point calculation. Drill the product rule and chain rule for composite functions.
Q3738%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to set up the optimization problem or express area in terms of one variable. Re-teach constraint optimization and substitution methods.
Q3712%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the setup right but make arithmetic errors in simplifying expressions or solving equations. Practice algebraic manipulation.
Q370%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand what needs to be optimized. Emphasize careful reading of optimization problems.
Q3856%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know how to check injectivity and equivalence relations but make errors in algebraic manipulation or incomplete proofs. Review step-by-step procedures for these concepts.
Q3822%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the definitions of injective functions or equivalence relations. Re-teach the fundamental concepts with clear examples.
Q3811%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the question requirements or focus on wrong parts of the problem. Emphasize careful reading and understanding of what needs to be proven.
Q3811%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method but make arithmetic errors in solving quadratic equations or algebraic simplifications. Practice computational skills.

🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.177%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to find derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions. Re-teach the differentiation formulas and substitution techniques step-by-step.
Q21.123%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the methods but make errors in applying chain rule or parametric differentiation steps. Drill the procedural steps for finding second derivatives.
Q21.276%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in executing steps like forgetting to divide by dx/dθ when computing second derivative or incorrect application of chain rule. Drill the complete formula for d²y/dx² in parametric form.
Q21.219%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the formula for second derivative in parametric form. Re-teach that d²y/dx² = (d/dθ(dy/dx))/(dx/dθ) step-by-step.
Q21.25%Read Questions WrongStudents misread y = sin³θ as y = a sin³θ, introducing an extra 'a' coefficient. Emphasize careful reading of given parametric equations.
Q2290%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to use cross product properties to prove vector parallelism. Re-teach the relationship between cross product being zero and parallel vectors.
Q225%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the approach but make errors in distributive law application for cross products. Drill the cross product algebra properties.
Q225%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in the final steps. Practice cross product calculations carefully.
Q2389%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use trigonometric identities to simplify expressions with sin⁻¹((sinx + cosx)/√2). Re-teach the sin(A+B) expansion and how 1/√2 = sin(π/4) = cos(π/4).
Q2311%Read Questions WrongStudents over-complicate the problem by considering principal value ranges when the question asks for direct simplification. Clarify that standard simplification is required unless explicitly asked for domain restrictions.
Q2453%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply continuity conditions at x=0. Re-teach that continuity requires LHL = RHL and how to evaluate limits of piecewise functions.
Q2426%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the continuity method but make errors in applying L'Hôpital's rule or setting up limit equations. Practice the systematic steps for continuity problems.
Q2416%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors when computing p = -1/2. Check calculation steps more carefully.
Q245%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the piecewise function definition or continuity interval. Emphasize careful reading of function domains.
Q25.152%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the substitution method but make errors in executing the steps correctly. Practice step-by-step substitution with careful algebraic manipulation.
Q25.124%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the integration by substitution method. Re-teach the fundamental technique of substitution in integration.
Q25.112%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in computation. Practice careful calculation steps in integration.
Q25.112%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand what the question is asking for. Clarify the difference between integration problems and area calculation problems.
Q25.256%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the integration method but make errors in setting up limits or splitting integrals incorrectly. Practice identifying where the line crosses the x-axis and setting proper integration bounds.
Q25.225%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the integral correctly but make arithmetic errors when evaluating limits or simplifying fractions. Drill basic fraction operations and substitution calculations.
Q25.219%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand that area below x-axis needs absolute value treatment or confuse the integration setup. Re-teach the concept of area between curves and x-axis.
Q2658%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to calculate 'at least 2' probability using independent events. Re-teach the concept of combining cases: exactly 2 hit + all 3 hit.
Q2621%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in setting up cases or miss including all 3 hitting. Drill the complete case enumeration.
Q2621%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the approach right but make arithmetic errors in fraction calculations. Practice fraction operations and LCM calculations.
Q2750%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to relate median to side length in an equilateral triangle or how to set up the rate of change equation. Re-teach the geometric relationships and chain rule applications.
Q2737%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the relationship between median and side but make errors in applying the chain rule or setting up the differentiation correctly. Practice more chain rule problems with geometric applications.
Q2713%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method and steps right but make arithmetic errors in the final calculations. Review basic algebraic manipulation and fraction operations.
Q28.173%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to differentiate implicit functions involving exponential and logarithmic expressions. Re-teach implicit differentiation and logarithmic differentiation methods.
Q28.127%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differentiation method but make algebraic errors when collecting dy/dx terms or simplifying expressions. Practice step-by-step algebraic manipulation.
Q28.283%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the implicit differentiation method but make errors in algebraic manipulation and simplification steps. Practice step-by-step algebraic manipulation.
Q28.217%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the implicit differentiation technique for parametric equations. Re-teach the fundamental method.
Q29.150%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the method for finding equations of lines in 3D geometry. Re-teach the fundamental concepts of vector form and Cartesian form of line equations.
Q29.125%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the question requirements and solve for different parameters than asked. Clarify what the question is specifically asking for.
Q29.125%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method but make arithmetic errors in calculations. Practice computational accuracy and double-check calculations.
Q29.10%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but execute the steps incorrectly. Drill the proper sequence of steps for solving 3D geometry problems.
Q29.250%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the method for finding equations of lines in 3D geometry. Re-teach the fundamental concepts of vector form and Cartesian form of line equations.
Q29.225%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the question requirements and solve for different parameters than asked. Clarify what the question is specifically asking for.
Q29.225%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method but make arithmetic errors in calculations. Practice computational accuracy and double-check calculations.
Q29.20%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but execute the steps incorrectly. Drill the proper sequence of steps for solving 3D geometry problems.
Q3043%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value equations or set up integration for area between curves. Re-teach modulus function properties and area calculation methods.
Q3029%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the integration method but make errors in setting up limits or miss the symmetry requirement. Drill the proper setup for absolute value curves.
Q3028%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up integration correctly but make arithmetic errors in evaluation or miss doubling for symmetry. Practice integration evaluation and final answer checks.
Q3191%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in finding corner points, evaluating objective function, or handling unbounded regions. Review systematic corner point identification and unbounded region analysis.
Q319%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the basic graphical method for linear programming. Re-teach constraint graphing, feasible region identification, and corner point evaluation.
Q3293%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to find equations of lines through origin making a specific angle with a given line. Re-teach the approach of using direction ratios and angle formula systematically.
Q327%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the angle formula but make errors in algebraic manipulation when deriving the condition. Practice solving the quadratic constraints step by step.
Q3350%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the inverse formula and property (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ but make errors in cofactor calculations, sign errors, or matrix multiplication steps. Practice step-by-step verification of each cofactor and multiplication.
Q3327%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in determinant calculation, cofactor computation, or final matrix multiplication. Emphasize careful arithmetic checking.
Q3314%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the reverse law (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ or confuse it with direct multiplication. Re-teach the fundamental property of inverse matrices.
Q339%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what needs to be found or use wrong approach like finding AB instead of (AB)⁻¹. Emphasize careful reading of the question requirements.
Q34.150%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the linear differential equation method right but mess up integration by parts or arithmetic. Practice integration techniques step-by-step.
Q34.133%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to recognize or solve linear differential equations. Re-teach the standard form dy/dx + Py = Q and integrating factor method.
Q34.117%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but skip steps or use wrong procedures. Drill the complete sequence: product rule, rearrange, find integrating factor, multiply, integrate.
Q34.250%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the linear differential equation method right but mess up integration by parts or arithmetic. Practice integration techniques step-by-step.
Q34.233%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to recognize or solve linear differential equations. Re-teach the standard form dy/dx + Py = Q and integrating factor method.
Q34.217%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but skip steps or use wrong procedures. Drill the complete sequence: product rule, rearrange, find integrating factor, multiply, integrate.
Q35.167%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply partial fraction decomposition to rational functions. Re-teach the method of partial fractions step-by-step.
Q35.133%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know partial fractions but make errors in setting up coefficients or factorization. Drill the systematic approach to finding constants.
Q35.256%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the property ∫₀^π f(x)dx = ∫₀^π f(π-x)dx but make errors in limit evaluation or substitution handling. Practice the complete integration process step-by-step.
Q35.233%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the King's property for definite integrals or how to apply symmetry properties. Re-teach the fundamental properties of definite integrals.
Q35.211%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in limit evaluation or final computation. Practice careful calculation of inverse trigonometric limits.
Q3650%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply Bayes' theorem and conditional probability concepts properly. Re-teach the fundamental formulas and when to use each approach.
Q3625%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the concepts but make arithmetic errors in fraction calculations and probability computations. Practice more calculation drills.
Q3619%Read Questions WrongStudents misread what probability they need to find, especially confusing conditional probability directions. Clarify question requirements before solving.
Q366%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formulas but apply them in wrong sequence or skip essential steps. Drill the step-by-step procedure for Bayes' theorem problems.
Q3750%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up the area function for a rectangle inscribed in an ellipse. Re-teach the relationship between ellipse equation and area optimization.
Q3733%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the differentiation steps right but make arithmetic errors in simplification. Practice algebraic manipulation and derivative calculations.
Q3717%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the optimization method but skip critical steps in finding the derivative. Drill the complete differentiation process for area functions.
Q3850%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand what injective and surjective mean, providing incomplete or incorrect reasoning. Clarify the formal definitions of one-one and onto functions.
Q3833%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to properly test for injective and surjective properties using formal methods. Re-teach the step-by-step approach for proving injectivity and surjectivity.
Q3817%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the general approach but make errors in algebraic manipulation when solving the injective test. Practice factoring and algebraic simplification.
