🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.183%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the basic derivative formula for inverse cosine functions. Re-teach the formula d/dx[cos⁻¹(u)] = -1/√(1-u²) × du/dx step-by-step.
Q21.117%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the derivative formula but make errors in applying the chain rule correctly. Practice more examples of chain rule with inverse trigonometric functions.
Q21.291%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the parametric differentiation method but make errors in applying the chain rule for second derivative or substituting values. Review the formula d²y/dx² = d/dx(dy/dx) and practice step-by-step calculations.
Q21.29%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship between dy/dx and tan θ for parametric equations. Re-teach how dy/dx = (dy/dθ)/(dx/dθ) simplifies for these specific parametric forms.
Q2283%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to manipulate cross product equations to prove vector parallelism. Re-teach vector algebra properties and cross product relationships.
Q2217%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know some vector properties but make logical errors in connecting cross products to parallelism. Practice step-by-step proof techniques.
Q23100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply trigonometric identities to simplify inverse trigonometric expressions. Re-teach the angle sum formula and how 1/√2 = sin(π/4) = cos(π/4).
Q2450%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply continuity conditions to piecewise functions. Re-teach the concept that left and right limits must be equal at boundary points.
Q2425%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the continuity method but make arithmetic errors when evaluating limits or solving for p. Practice limit calculations and algebraic manipulation.
Q2417%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know continuity conditions but apply L'Hôpital's rule incorrectly or mix up left and right limits. Drill the proper sequence for checking continuity.
Q248%Read Questions WrongStudents misidentified which point to check for continuity. Emphasize reading the function definition carefully to identify boundary points.
Q25.1100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply integration by substitution method. Re-teach the substitution technique step-by-step with practice problems.
Q25.242%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand when to consider absolute values or split integrals for area calculations. Re-teach the concept of area under curves when the function crosses the x-axis.
Q25.233%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the integration method but make errors in setting up the integral limits or incorrectly split the integral. Practice setting up area problems with proper limits.
Q25.217%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the integral correctly but make arithmetic errors in evaluation. Practice basic algebraic manipulation and substitution.
Q25.28%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the problem and solve a different area problem entirely. Emphasize careful reading of the given function and limits.
Q2673%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the principle of inclusion-exclusion for 'at least two' events. Re-teach how to break down 'at least two' into mutually exclusive cases: exactly two + exactly three.
Q2618%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the approach but don't account for complementary probabilities or miss cases. Practice identifying all scenarios for 'at least two' outcomes.
Q269%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in fraction operations. Drill fraction multiplication and addition with common denominators.
Q2767%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the relationship between median and side but make errors in differentiation or setting up equations. Review the chain rule and related rates methodology.
Q2717%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what quantity is changing at the given rate. Emphasize careful reading of rate of change problems.
Q2716%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship between median and side of equilateral triangle. Review geometric properties before tackling rate problems.
Q28.150%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the logarithmic differentiation method but make errors in algebraic manipulation when isolating dy/dx. Practice step-by-step algebraic simplification.
Q28.133%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the proper application of logarithmic properties or implicit differentiation rules. Re-teach logarithmic differentiation method.
Q28.117%Make Calculation MistakesStudents follow the correct method but make arithmetic errors in fraction manipulation. Practice algebraic fraction operations.
Q28.2100%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know implicit differentiation but make errors in applying product rule and chain rule correctly. Drill the step-by-step process for differentiating implicit equations.
Q29.150%Read Questions WrongMisread the question or didn't understand what was being asked.
Q29.150%Make Mistakes in StepsKnows what to do but does steps in wrong order or skips critical parts.
Q29.250%Make Calculation MistakesUnderstanding is fine, but simple arithmetic or algebra mistakes mess up the answer.
Q29.233%Make Mistakes in StepsKnows what to do but does steps in wrong order or skips critical parts.
Q29.217%Foundational GapsDon't understand the method. He's guessing or using wrong formulas. Doesn't get the "why" behind the math.
Q3060%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value in area calculations. Re-teach the concept that |y| creates two cases and requires splitting the integral.
Q3040%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in setting up limits or handling the absolute value cases. Practice identifying correct integration bounds for piecewise functions.
Q3159%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the linear programming method but make errors in finding corner points or checking unbounded regions. Review intersection calculations and unbounded region analysis.
Q3125%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but mess up arithmetic when calculating corner points or objective function values. Practice intersection calculations and substitution.
Q318%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the complete graphical method for linear programming. Re-teach the systematic approach from graphing constraints to finding optimal values.
Q318%Read Questions WrongStudents misread constraints or objective function. Emphasize careful reading of inequality signs and coefficient values.
Q3290%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to find the angle between two lines in 3D using direction vectors and the dot product formula. Re-teach the fundamental concepts of direction vectors and angle between lines.
Q3210%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the angle formula but use wrong approach for 3D lines. Clarify that the given formula is for 2D lines and teach the correct 3D vector approach.
Q3350%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for finding matrix inverse but make errors in the order of multiplication or transpose steps. Review the proper sequence: (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹.
Q3325%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method and steps correct but make arithmetic errors in matrix multiplication or cofactor calculations. Practice matrix multiplication drills.
Q3317%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ and attempt incorrect approaches like finding (AB) first. Re-teach inverse properties for matrix products.
Q338%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the matrix values or confuse the given matrices. Emphasize careful reading and identification of given information.
Q34.150%Make Mistakes in StepsKnows what to do but does steps in wrong order or skips critical parts.
Q34.120%Make Calculation MistakesUnderstanding is fine, but simple arithmetic or algebra mistakes mess up the answer.
Q34.120%Read Questions WrongMisread the question or didn't understand what was being asked.
Q34.110%Foundational GapsDon't understand the method. He's guessing or using wrong formulas. Doesn't get the "why" behind the math.
Q34.250%Foundational GapsDon't understand the method. He's guessing or using wrong formulas. Doesn't get the "why" behind the math.
Q34.233%Make Mistakes in StepsKnows what to do but does steps in wrong order or skips critical parts.
Q34.217%Make Calculation MistakesUnderstanding is fine, but simple arithmetic or algebra mistakes mess up the answer.
Q35.150%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know partial fractions method but make errors in setting up equations or solving for coefficients. Review the systematic approach for finding A, B, C values.
Q35.133%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to factor the cubic polynomial correctly or set up proper partial fraction decomposition. Re-teach factorization techniques and partial fraction setup.
Q35.117%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors when solving the system of equations for coefficients. Practice solving linear systems carefully.
Q35.257%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the properties of definite integrals but make errors in applying them correctly. Review the step-by-step application of integration properties.
Q35.243%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental properties of definite integrals. Re-teach the core properties and their applications from basics.
Q3640%Read Questions WrongMisread the question or didn't understand what was being asked.
Q3640%Foundational GapsDon't understand the method. He's guessing or using wrong formulas. Doesn't get the "why" behind the math.
Q3610%Make Calculation MistakesUnderstanding is fine, but simple arithmetic or algebra mistakes mess up the answer.
Q3610%Make Mistakes in StepsKnows what to do but does steps in wrong order or skips critical parts.
Q3775%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up optimization problems with elliptical constraints. Re-teach the method of expressing area as a function of one variable using ellipse equation.
Q3725%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the setup but make algebraic errors when differentiating the area function. Practice differentiation of composite functions and product rule.
Q3840%Make Mistakes in StepsKnows what to do but does steps in wrong order or skips critical parts.
Q3840%Foundational GapsDon't understand the method. He's guessing or using wrong formulas. Doesn't get the "why" behind the math.

🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.189%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to find derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions. Re-teach the derivative formulas and chain rule application.
Q21.111%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the concept but make errors in applying substitutions or simplifying expressions. Practice step-by-step differentiation.
Q21.284%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the parametric differentiation method but make errors in applying the chain rule or substituting dθ/dx correctly. Review the formula d²y/dx² = (d/dθ(dy/dx)) ÷ (dx/dθ).
Q21.211%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method and steps right but make arithmetic errors when simplifying trigonometric expressions. Practice trigonometric calculations and substitution at θ = π/6.
Q21.25%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the parametric differentiation method for finding second derivatives. Re-teach the complete process step-by-step with clear examples.
Q22100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use the distributive property of cross products to manipulate the given vector equations. Re-teach vector algebra properties and proof techniques.
Q2373%Foundational GapsStudents don't recognize the trigonometric identity pattern (sinx + cosx)/√2 = sin(x + π/4). Re-teach compound angle formulas and their applications in inverse trigonometric functions.
Q2318%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying trigonometric identities or miss the principal value domain restrictions. Practice step-by-step simplification of inverse trigonometric expressions.
Q239%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make small errors in trigonometric value substitutions. Drill basic trigonometric values like sin(π/4) = cos(π/4) = 1/√2.
Q2450%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the continuity method but make errors in algebraic manipulation or limit evaluation. Practice step-by-step limit calculations.
Q2436%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand continuity conditions or how to set up LHL = RHL = f(0). Re-teach continuity fundamentals.
Q247%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in final calculations. Practice computational accuracy.
Q247%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the problem setup or continuity requirements. Emphasize careful reading of function definitions.
Q25.178%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply integration by substitution for logarithmic functions. Re-teach the substitution method with log x = t and proper handling of dx.
Q25.122%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the substitution method but make errors in the integration steps or forget to substitute back properly. Drill the complete procedure.
Q25.250%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to find area by integration but make errors in setting up correct limits or handling absolute values for area below x-axis. Practice identifying where function changes sign and using correct integration bounds.
Q25.225%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand that area under x-axis must be taken as absolute value. Re-teach the concept that area is always positive and requires splitting integrals at x-intercepts.
Q25.213%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the question requirements or confused it with a different problem type. Clarify what exactly needs to be calculated for area under curves.
Q25.212%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the problem correctly but make arithmetic errors in evaluating definite integrals. Practice careful computation of integration bounds and fraction arithmetic.
Q2693%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to calculate probability of at least two people hitting the target. Re-teach the complement approach and mutually exclusive events.
Q267%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in calculating individual probabilities. Review step-by-step probability calculations.
Q2771%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to relate median length to side length in equilateral triangles or how to apply related rates. Re-teach the relationship between median and side in equilateral triangles, then practice differentiation with respect to time.
Q2729%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the basic relationship but make errors in differentiation or algebraic manipulation. Practice step-by-step differentiation of related rate problems.
Q28.164%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply logarithmic differentiation to implicit functions. Re-teach the method of taking logarithms of both sides before differentiating.
Q28.136%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to take logarithms but make errors in the differentiation steps or algebraic manipulation. Practice step-by-step logarithmic differentiation.
Q28.256%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know implicit differentiation method but make errors in algebraic manipulation when isolating dy/dx. Practice rearranging complex expressions with multiple dy/dx terms.
Q28.244%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand implicit differentiation for trigonometric functions or product rule application. Re-teach chain rule and product rule for composite trig functions.
Q29.150%Read Questions WrongStudents solve for shortest distance between skew lines instead of distance from point to line. Review the difference between these two concepts.
Q29.150%Foundational GapsStudents lack understanding of 3D line equations and perpendicular distance formulas. Re-teach the fundamental concepts and methods.
Q29.243%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the correct formula for shortest distance between skew lines. Re-teach the vector formula for distance between two skew lines.
Q29.243%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the method but make arithmetic errors in cross products and dot products. Practice vector calculations step-by-step.
Q29.214%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula but apply it incorrectly or skip critical steps. Emphasize proper sequence of operations.
Q3067%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents understand the concept of absolute value and area calculation but make errors in setting up proper limits or integrating correctly. Review integration techniques and limit determination.
Q3022%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value equations or set up area integrals properly. Re-teach the relationship between |y| and piecewise functions.
Q3011%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the equation x + 2|y| = 1 or the area calculation requirements. Emphasize careful reading of absolute value notation.
Q3172%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in finding corner points or evaluating the objective function. Practice intersection calculations and systematic evaluation.
Q3116%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the complete graphical method for linear programming. Re-teach feasible region identification and corner point evaluation.
Q3112%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in calculating intersection points or objective function values. Practice fraction arithmetic.
Q3250%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to find angle between lines using dot product formula. Re-teach the concept of direction ratios and angle formula.
Q3244%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in setting up equations or algebraic manipulation. Practice the step-by-step procedure.
Q326%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in calculations. Focus on careful computation practice.
Q3355%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ but make errors in computing adjoint or matrix multiplication. Review step-by-step cofactor calculation and matrix multiplication rules.
Q3328%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in determinant calculation or matrix multiplication. Practice basic arithmetic operations with matrices.
Q3311%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship between adjoint, determinant and inverse matrices. Re-teach the fundamental concepts of matrix inverse.
Q336%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the matrix elements or formula. Emphasize careful reading of given matrices and formulas.
Q34.175%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up differential equations for growth/decay problems. Re-teach the exponential growth model dN/dt = kN and its applications.
Q34.125%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the differential equation approach but make arithmetic errors in logarithmic calculations. Practice solving exponential equations with logarithms.
Q34.280%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method of solving linear differential equations but make errors in rearranging terms or setting up the standard form. Drill the systematic approach to converting to dy/dx + Py = Q form.
Q34.220%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the differential equation or substituted incorrect terms. Emphasize careful reading and verification of the given equation before proceeding.
Q35.156%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to set up partial fraction decomposition for rational functions. Re-teach the method of breaking down complex fractions into simpler forms.
Q35.144%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know partial fractions but make errors in coefficient calculation or algebraic manipulation. Practice systematic approaches to finding A, B, C values.
Q35.280%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply King's property for definite integrals with trigonometric expressions. Re-teach the property and practice with similar problems.
Q35.220%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying trigonometric identities or substitution. Drill the step-by-step procedure.
Q3644%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the law of total probability and Bayes' theorem for conditional probability problems. Re-teach these fundamental concepts with step-by-step examples.
Q3622%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formulas but apply them incorrectly or skip crucial steps in Bayes' theorem calculations. Drill the proper sequence of steps.
Q3622%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method but make arithmetic errors when multiplying probabilities and fractions. Practice computational accuracy.
Q3612%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what the question is asking for in conditional probability contexts. Emphasize careful reading of probability statements.
Q3763%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to set up area functions for rectangles inscribed in ellipses. Re-teach the method of expressing area in terms of one variable using ellipse equation.
Q3725%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in differentiation or critical point calculation. Drill the steps for finding derivatives of composite functions.
Q3712%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what to find in maxima-minima problems. Clarify that they need to find maximum area, not solve general ellipse properties.
Q3850%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of injectivity and surjectivity, or how to prove equivalence relations. Re-teach function properties and relation types step-by-step.
Q3833%Read Questions WrongStudents misread parts of the question or focused on wrong aspects. Emphasize careful reading and understanding what needs to be proven.
Q3817%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in algebraic manipulation or logical steps. Practice systematic proof techniques.

🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.180%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to differentiate inverse trigonometric functions using chain rule. Re-teach the derivatives of inverse trig functions and chain rule application.
Q21.113%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying chain rule or simplifying expressions. Practice step-by-step differentiation with feedback.
Q21.17%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the function form or variable. Emphasize careful reading of the given function before starting differentiation.
Q21.267%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the parametric differentiation method but make errors in applying chain rule for second derivative or incorrect algebraic manipulation. Drill the complete process step-by-step.
Q21.227%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand parametric differentiation or confuse the given equations. Re-teach the concept of finding dy/dx and d²y/dx² for parametric equations.
Q21.26%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in trigonometric calculations. Practice trigonometric computations.
Q2284%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to use cross product properties to prove vector parallelism. Re-teach the method of expanding cross products and using distributive properties.
Q2211%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the correct approach but make errors in manipulating the cross product expressions. Practice step-by-step algebraic manipulation of vector equations.
Q225%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make sign errors when applying cross product properties. Drill the anti-commutative property of cross products.
Q23100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use the sin(A+B) expansion formula to simplify (sin x + cos x)/√2. Re-teach the technique of writing 1/√2 as sin(π/4) = cos(π/4).
Q2450%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand continuity conditions or how to set up LHL = RHL equations. Re-teach continuity definition and limit evaluation methods.
Q2442%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the continuity approach but make errors in limit calculations or algebraic manipulation. Drill rationalization techniques and limit evaluation steps.
Q248%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in squaring or sign handling. Practice basic algebraic computations.
Q25.165%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply integration by substitution method. Re-teach the substitution technique step-by-step.
Q25.121%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the substitution method but make errors in transforming dx or manipulating the integrand. Practice substitution steps carefully.
Q25.114%Read Questions WrongStudents solve a different integral or misread the function. Clarify what exactly needs to be integrated.
Q25.262%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the integration method but make errors in handling absolute values or setting up limits incorrectly. Review when to split integrals and proper limit handling.
Q25.223%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the integral correctly but make arithmetic errors in evaluation or substitution. Practice careful calculation steps.
Q25.215%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of area under curves or integration setup. Re-teach basic integration for area calculation.
Q2653%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of 'at least two' events and how to calculate it using complementary cases. Re-teach the systematic approach for 'at least' problems.
Q2642%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but miss including all three cases (exactly 2) or make errors in case identification. Drill the systematic enumeration of all possible cases.
Q265%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method and steps right but make arithmetic errors in fraction calculations. Practice fraction multiplication and addition.
Q2757%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the correct formula for median of equilateral triangle or confuse it with other properties. Re-teach that median = (√3/2)a where a is the side.
Q2729%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the relationship but make errors in differentiation steps or algebraic manipulation. Practice chain rule and related rates systematically.
Q2714%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but use wrong formula values like m = √3a/4 instead of √3a/2. Drill the exact formulas for equilateral triangle properties.
Q28.154%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the logarithmic differentiation method but make algebraic errors when simplifying fractions or collecting terms. Practice algebraic manipulation of fractions.
Q28.146%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand logarithmic differentiation for implicit functions or confuse partial derivatives with total derivatives. Re-teach logarithmic differentiation method step-by-step.
Q28.260%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand implicit differentiation techniques for parametric equations. Re-teach the method of differentiating both sides with respect to x and collecting dy/dx terms.
Q28.230%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know implicit differentiation but make errors in applying chain rule or collecting terms. Practice step-by-step differentiation of trigonometric functions.
Q28.210%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in simplifying trigonometric expressions. Review trigonometric identities and algebraic manipulation.
Q29.150%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply the shortest distance formula between skew lines. Re-teach the vector formula and its components.
Q29.133%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the formula but make arithmetic errors in cross product calculations or dot product operations. Practice vector computations.
Q29.117%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what the question is asking for in the context of line equations. Clarify problem requirements before solving.
Q29.250%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply the shortest distance formula between skew lines. Re-teach the vector formula and its components.
Q29.233%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the formula but make arithmetic errors in cross product calculations or dot product operations. Practice vector computations.
Q29.217%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what the question is asking for in the context of line equations. Clarify problem requirements before solving.
Q3047%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value equations x + 2|y| = 1 and split into cases for y > 0 and y < 0. Re-teach absolute value properties and case-by-case analysis.
Q3040%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in setting up integration limits or choosing wrong variables. Drill the systematic approach to finding intersection points and setting up integrals.
Q3013%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the setup right but make arithmetic errors in integration or final calculations. Practice integration techniques and careful computation.
Q3164%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in finding intersection points or identifying the correct feasible region. Drill the step-by-step process for finding corner points.
Q3116%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the graphical method for linear programming problems. Re-teach the complete method including constraint plotting and feasible region identification.
Q3110%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method but make arithmetic errors when calculating intersection points or evaluating the objective function. Practice computational accuracy.
Q3110%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the constraints or objective function. Emphasize careful reading of linear programming problems and identifying what needs to be minimized or maximized.
Q3275%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to find the equation of a line through the origin that makes a specific angle with a given line. Re-teach the angle between lines formula and its application to finding unknown lines.
Q3225%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the angle formula but make errors in setting up equations or algebraic manipulation. Practice step-by-step problem solving with careful equation setup.
Q3345%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in cofactor calculations or matrix multiplication steps. Drill the procedural steps systematically.
Q3335%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in determinant calculation or matrix multiplication. Practice computational accuracy.
Q3320%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the property (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ or how to find matrix inverse. Re-teach the fundamental concepts.
Q34.155%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the product rule for differentiation but make errors in applying it to d/dx(xy). Review step-by-step application of product rule.
Q34.127%Foundational GapsStudents don't recognize this as a linear differential equation form. Re-teach identification and standard form dy/dx + Py = Q.
Q34.19%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the given equation or substitute incorrect values. Emphasize careful reading of differential equations.
Q34.19%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in simplification. Practice algebraic manipulation.
Q34.255%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the product rule for differentiation but make errors in applying it to d/dx(xy). Review step-by-step application of product rule.
Q34.227%Foundational GapsStudents don't recognize this as a linear differential equation form. Re-teach identification and standard form dy/dx + Py = Q.
Q34.29%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the given equation or substitute incorrect values. Emphasize careful reading of differential equations.
Q34.29%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in simplification. Practice algebraic manipulation.
Q35.148%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use partial fractions for rational function integration. Re-teach partial fraction decomposition step-by-step.
Q35.143%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know partial fractions method but make errors in factorization or setting up the decomposition. Drill the systematic approach.
Q35.19%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in solving for constants. Practice algebraic manipulation.
Q35.250%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the property method but make calculation errors in the integration steps or final evaluation. Practice more integration of trigonometric functions with careful arithmetic.
Q35.238%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to use the property f(x) = f(π-x) but make errors in applying the substitution or combining the integrals correctly. Drill the step-by-step application of definite integral properties.
Q35.212%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply properties of definite integrals for this type of problem. Re-teach the property ∫₀^π f(x)dx = ∫₀^π f(π-x)dx with examples.
Q3640%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of conditional probability and Bayes' theorem. Re-teach the fundamental formulas and when to apply them.
Q3627%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying Bayes' theorem formula. Practice step-by-step application of the formula.
Q3620%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the approach right but make arithmetic errors in probability calculations. Drill basic fraction and decimal operations.
Q3613%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what probability is being asked for in each subpart. Clarify the difference between conditional and joint probabilities.
Q3740%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up optimization problems with constraints. They write area formula but don't know how to use the ellipse equation as constraint for maximization.
Q3730%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the method but make arithmetic errors in differentiation and algebraic manipulation. Practice derivative calculations and constraint substitution.
Q3720%Read Questions WrongStudents confuse the ellipse equation format and use wrong variable substitutions. Clarify the relationship between rectangle dimensions and ellipse coordinates.
Q3710%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents attempt the optimization process but use incorrect equation setup for the ellipse constraint. Review the standard form of ellipse equation.
Q3833%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what needs to be proven for injectivity and surjectivity. Clarify that they need to check if every element in codomain has a pre-image for surjectivity.
Q3833%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the definitions of injective and surjective functions. Re-teach one-to-one and onto concepts with clear examples.
Q3817%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but skip crucial algebraic steps in proving injectivity. Practice step-by-step algebraic manipulation.
Q3817%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the approach right but make arithmetic errors in factorization and algebraic simplification. Practice basic algebra skills.

🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.188%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to differentiate inverse trigonometric functions using chain rule. Re-teach the fundamental formulas and step-by-step application.
Q21.112%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differentiation method but make errors in applying the chain rule correctly. Practice more step-by-step examples.
Q21.279%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the parametric differentiation method but make errors in applying the chain rule for second derivative. Practice the formula d²y/dx² = (d/dθ(dy/dx)) ÷ (dx/dθ).
Q21.214%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the correct approach for finding second derivative of parametric equations. Re-teach the chain rule method step-by-step.
Q21.27%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in trigonometric calculations. Practice trigonometric values and algebraic simplification.
Q2285%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the key insight that parallel vectors have zero cross product. Re-teach the condition for parallel vectors: (a - d) × (b - c) = 0.
Q2215%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the parallel vector condition but make errors in algebraic manipulation. Practice expanding cross products and applying given conditions systematically.
Q2388%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to recognize sin⁻¹(sinx·cos(π/4) + cosx·sin(π/4)) as sin⁻¹(sin(x + π/4)). Re-teach trigonometric identities for compound angles.
Q236%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents get the trigonometric identity setup right but make errors in the final simplification steps. Practice more worked examples.
Q236%Read Questions WrongStudents substitute specific values for x instead of solving the general expression. Clarify that this requires algebraic manipulation, not numerical substitution.
Q2445%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the continuity method but make errors in executing steps like using wrong denominators or incorrect limit calculations. Drill the step-by-step process for checking continuity.
Q2439%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of continuity at a point or use wrong approaches like checking continuity at wrong points. Re-teach the fundamental definition of continuity.
Q248%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the problem and check continuity at multiple points instead of finding the value of p. Clarify what the question is asking for.
Q248%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in calculating limits or final value of p. Practice computational accuracy.
Q25.156%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply substitution method correctly for logarithmic integration. Re-teach substitution technique with step-by-step examples.
Q25.133%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the substitution method but make errors in the algebraic manipulation after substitution. Drill the steps of simplifying rational expressions.
Q25.111%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method and steps right but make arithmetic errors in final computation. Practice careful calculation.
Q25.250%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to find area under curves bounded by lines and axes. They either miss the concept of absolute area or confuse with other integration concepts. Re-teach the fundamental concept of area calculation using definite integrals.
Q25.230%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the integration method but make errors in setting up limits or handling negative areas. They miss the step of taking absolute values when the curve goes below x-axis. Practice problems with regions crossing the x-axis.
Q25.220%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the integral correctly but make arithmetic errors in evaluating the definite integral. Focus on careful substitution of limits and fraction arithmetic.
Q2678%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to calculate probability of 'at least 2 people' hitting target. Re-teach complement method and inclusion-exclusion principle for compound probability events.
Q2613%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in setting up the probability combinations. Practice identifying all possible cases for 'at least 2' scenarios.
Q269%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the approach right but make arithmetic errors in fraction operations. Drill fraction multiplication and addition with common denominators.
Q2748%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the relationship between median and side but make errors in differentiation steps or algebraic manipulation. Review chain rule and algebraic simplification.
Q2738%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship between median and side of equilateral triangle or confuse median with height. Re-teach median formula: median = (√3/2)a.
Q2714%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the setup right but make arithmetic errors when solving for da/dt. Practice algebraic manipulation with radicals.
Q28.155%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the proper approach for implicit differentiation of exponential functions. Re-teach logarithmic differentiation method step-by-step.
Q28.145%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the logarithmic differentiation approach but make algebraic manipulation errors. Practice rearranging terms and factoring dy/dx.
Q28.250%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know implicit differentiation but make errors in algebraic manipulation when collecting dy/dx terms or applying the chain rule. Practice step-by-step algebraic rearrangement.
Q28.250%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to properly apply implicit differentiation to trigonometric equations. Re-teach implicit differentiation with trigonometric functions and chain rule applications.
Q29.1100%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the shortest distance formula but make errors in vector calculations or component identification. Practice step-by-step vector operations and cross product calculations.
Q29.2100%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the shortest distance formula but make errors in vector calculations or component identification. Practice step-by-step vector operations and cross product calculations.
Q3054%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to set up the integral for area bounded by curves with absolute value. Re-teach how to handle |y| by splitting into cases and identifying correct limits.
Q3023%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in setting up the integral bounds or forget the factor of 2 for symmetry. Drill the steps for area calculation with absolute value functions.
Q3015%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the setup right but mess up integration or arithmetic. Practice basic integration of linear functions and fraction arithmetic.
Q308%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret which area to find or the bounds of integration. Emphasize careful reading of what region is being asked for.
Q3150%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in finding corner points or evaluating the objective function. Review intersection calculations and corner point identification.
Q3125%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the unbounded region concept or how to handle minimization in unbounded feasible regions. Re-teach unbounded region analysis and when minimum values exist.
Q3113%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in computing corner points or objective function values. Practice intersection calculations and fraction arithmetic.
Q3112%Read Questions WrongStudents misread constraints or the objective function. Emphasize careful reading of constraint inequalities and objective function signs.
Q3289%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to find angle between two lines using direction ratios. Re-teach the cosine formula for angle between lines.
Q3211%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying the parametric form or setting up equations. Practice step-by-step problem solving.
Q3350%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental property (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹. They attempt wrong formulas like A⁻¹B⁻¹ or compute AB first. Re-teach the matrix inverse multiplication property.
Q3333%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in cofactor signs, adjoint transpose, or matrix multiplication steps. Review step-by-step matrix operations carefully.
Q3317%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in determinant calculation or matrix multiplication. Practice computational accuracy.
Q34.175%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to set up differential equations for growth problems. Re-teach the standard form dP/dt = kP and how to solve it.
Q34.125%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differential equation setup but make errors in integration or applying boundary conditions. Practice step-by-step solution method.
Q34.290%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents make errors in the steps of solving the differential equation. They correctly identify it as a linear DE but make mistakes in applying the method or rearranging terms.
Q34.210%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method but make arithmetic errors in their calculations while solving the differential equation.
Q35.154%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to properly factor the denominator or set up partial fractions correctly. Re-teach factoring cubic polynomials and partial fraction decomposition methods.
Q35.131%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know partial fractions method but make errors in coefficient comparison or substitution steps. Drill the systematic approach for finding constants.
Q35.115%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the numerator or denominator coefficients in the integral. Emphasize careful reading and copying of the given expression.
Q35.264%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand properties of definite integrals for symmetric functions. Re-teach the property that ∫[0 to a] f(x)dx = ∫[0 to a] f(a-x)dx and how to use it to simplify integrals.
Q35.227%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the approach but make errors in applying the substitution or symmetry property correctly. Practice step-by-step application of definite integral properties.
Q35.29%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in the final calculation. Review computation of inverse trigonometric function limits and fraction simplification.
Q3675%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply Bayes' theorem and conditional probability formulas correctly. Re-teach the fundamental concepts with step-by-step examples.
Q3619%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the concepts but make errors in setting up the probability formulas or miss key steps in the calculation process. Practice more structured problem-solving approaches.
Q366%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method but make arithmetic errors when computing fractions and percentages. Practice computational accuracy with probability calculations.
Q3740%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up maxima-minima problems with constraints. Re-teach the method of expressing one variable in terms of another using the constraint.
Q3720%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the optimization method but make errors in setting up the area function or applying differentiation rules. Drill the step-by-step procedure for constrained optimization.
Q3720%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in differentiation or algebraic manipulation. Practice chain rule and product rule calculations.
Q3720%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand what needs to be optimized or how to interpret the geometric constraints. Clarify problem statement interpretation.
Q3846%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the proper method to prove injectivity using the definition f(x₁) = f(x₂) implies x₁ = x₂. Re-teach the fundamental approach to proving one-to-one functions.
Q3831%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the injectivity test method but make errors in algebraic manipulation or skip crucial steps in the proof. Practice step-by-step algebraic simplification.
Q3815%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand what needs to be proven or confuse the domain/codomain requirements. Clarify the difference between proving injectivity and surjectivity.
Q388%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in factoring or solving quadratic equations. Review basic algebraic operations.

🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.1100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the correct derivative formula for inverse cosine or make fundamental errors in applying chain rule and quotient rule. Re-teach derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions systematically.
Q21.250%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the correct formula for second derivative of parametric equations. Re-teach that d²y/dx² = (d/dθ)(dy/dx) ÷ (dx/dθ).
Q21.242%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying chain rule or substituting values at θ = π/6. Practice step-by-step differentiation.
Q21.28%Read Questions WrongStudents attempted a different approach or misunderstood the parametric form. Clarify the standard parametric differentiation method.
Q2267%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to manipulate vector cross products to prove parallelism. Re-teach cross product properties and parallel vector conditions.
Q2233%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know cross product properties but make errors in algebraic manipulation or logical flow. Practice step-by-step proof techniques.
Q2383%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use trigonometric identities to simplify expressions inside inverse trig functions. Re-teach the identity sin x + cos x = √2 sin(x + π/4).
Q2317%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents recognize the identity but make errors in applying it correctly. Drill the step-by-step process of using sin(A + B) = sin A cos B + cos A sin B.
Q2450%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to check LHL = RHL for continuity but make errors in limit evaluation or algebraic manipulation. Review limit calculation techniques and rationalization methods.
Q2450%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the condition for continuity of piecewise functions or confuse endpoint evaluation with limit evaluation. Re-teach continuity conditions for piecewise functions.
Q25.1100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply integration by substitution correctly for complex logarithmic expressions. Re-teach the substitution method step-by-step with similar problems.
Q25.240%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand that area below x-axis needs absolute value or splitting at x-intercept. Re-teach concept of signed vs unsigned area.
Q25.240%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to split integral but make errors in setting up limits or forget absolute value bars. Drill the step-by-step process.
Q25.220%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get method right but make arithmetic errors in evaluating definite integrals. Practice limit substitution calculations.
Q2687%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand that 'at least two' means exactly two OR all three hitting. They confuse independent events with conditional probability and use wrong formulas.
Q2613%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the correct approach for 'at least two' but make errors in setting up or combining the three cases properly.
Q2760%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the relationship between median and side of equilateral triangle but make errors in differentiation or algebraic manipulation. Drill the differentiation steps and emphasize careful algebraic work.
Q2730%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the geometric relationship between median and side in an equilateral triangle or confuse this problem with unrelated concepts. Re-teach the formula for median of equilateral triangle and its derivation.
Q2710%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in the final calculation. Practice computational skills with square roots and fractions.
Q28.164%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the logarithmic differentiation method for implicit differentiation. Re-teach taking log of both sides and applying differentiation rules.
Q28.136%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in algebraic manipulation or product rule application. Drill the step-by-step process of logarithmic differentiation.
Q28.260%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply implicit differentiation to trigonometric equations. Re-teach the chain rule and product rule for implicit differentiation step-by-step.
Q28.240%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differentiation rules but make errors in algebraic manipulation or substitution steps. Practice more problems involving trigonometric identities and algebraic simplification.
Q29.157%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the cross product and formula setup right but make arithmetic errors in final calculations. Practice computational accuracy with vector operations.
Q29.129%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the shortest distance formula but make errors in cross product calculations or component operations. Drill the step-by-step procedure.
Q29.114%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the formula for shortest distance between skew lines. Re-teach the concept and formula derivation.
Q29.257%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the cross product and formula setup right but make arithmetic errors in final calculations. Practice computational accuracy with vector operations.
Q29.229%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the shortest distance formula but make errors in cross product calculations or component operations. Drill the step-by-step procedure.
Q29.214%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the formula for shortest distance between skew lines. Re-teach the concept and formula derivation.
Q3077%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value functions in area problems. Re-teach how |y| creates symmetric regions and requires splitting the integral.
Q3015%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the setup but make errors in integral limits or miss the symmetry factor. Practice identifying correct bounds for absolute value curves.
Q308%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the integral correctly but make arithmetic errors in evaluation. Practice integration calculations with fractions.
Q3153%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in finding corner points or evaluating objective function values. Review intersection calculations and function evaluation steps.
Q3140%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand when linear programming problems have no minimum or don't recognize unbounded feasible regions. Re-teach the concept of bounded vs unbounded regions.
Q317%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in calculating objective function values. Practice computation of negative fractions.
Q3292%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the formula for angle between lines. They confuse direction vectors, incorrectly apply the dot product formula, or use wrong coordinates. Re-teach the concept: cos θ = |b₁·b₂|/(|b₁||b₂|).
Q328%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but fail to complete the solution properly. They start correctly but don't follow through with all required steps to find the equation of lines.
Q3347%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in matrix multiplication or cofactor calculations. Drill the step-by-step process for finding inverse matrices.
Q3327%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in cofactors or matrix operations. Practice computational accuracy.
Q3326%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the property (AB)^-1 = B^-1 A^-1 or confuse the order. Re-teach inverse properties of matrix multiplication.
Q34.150%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the linear differential equation method but make errors in applying product rule or setting up the standard form. Review step-by-step procedure for solving dy/dx + Py = Q.
Q34.138%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in integration by parts or computing the integrating factor. Practice integration techniques for x²sinx and x²logx.
Q34.112%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the differential equation structure and apply wrong coefficient for P in the standard form. Emphasize careful identification of coefficients in dy/dx + Py = Q.
Q34.250%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the linear differential equation method but make errors in applying product rule or setting up the standard form. Review step-by-step procedure for solving dy/dx + Py = Q.
Q34.238%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in integration by parts or computing the integrating factor. Practice integration techniques for x²sinx and x²logx.
Q34.212%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the differential equation structure and apply wrong coefficient for P in the standard form. Emphasize careful identification of coefficients in dy/dx + Py = Q.
Q35.170%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to factorize the denominator or set up partial fractions for rational function integration. Re-teach factorization techniques and partial fraction decomposition methods step-by-step.
Q35.130%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up partial fractions correctly but make arithmetic errors when solving for constants or integrating individual terms. Practice coefficient comparison and basic integration formulas.
Q35.275%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply properties of definite integrals to evaluate this integral. Re-teach the property ∫₀ᵃ f(x)dx = ∫₀ᵃ f(a-x)dx and its application.
Q35.225%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstood what the question was asking for. Review the question requirements and emphasize careful reading of the problem statement.
Q3656%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply total probability law or Bayes' theorem properly. They misinterpret given probabilities and add them directly without considering conditional relationships. Re-teach conditional probability and the multiplication rule.
Q3631%Read Questions WrongStudents confuse production percentages with defect rates and mix up conditional probability notation. They read P(D|A) as P(A|D) or interpret machine output rates as defect probabilities. Clarify probability notation and problem setup.
Q3613%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the correct formulas but make arithmetic errors when multiplying fractions or converting percentages. Practice fraction multiplication and percentage calculations.
Q3760%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to find area as length × breadth and differentiate, but make algebraic errors in solving for critical points. Practice solving the derivative equation systematically.
Q3720%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand the problem setup and don't use the ellipse constraint properly. Clarify how to inscribe a rectangle in an ellipse.
Q3720%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand optimization problems and confuse area with perimeter. Re-teach the maxima-minima method for constrained optimization.
Q3850%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for proving injectivity but make logical errors in algebraic steps. Review step-by-step proof techniques for one-one functions.
Q3825%Read Questions WrongStudents attempt wrong parts or misunderstand the question requirements. Clarify what needs to be proven for each subpart.
Q3817%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the definitions of injective and surjective functions. Re-teach fundamental concepts of function types.
Q388%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in algebraic manipulation. Practice algebraic simplification.

🧩 Struggle Gaps
Q%Sub-topicDescription
Q21.158%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differentiation rules for inverse trigonometric functions but make errors in applying the chain rule or simplification steps. Drill the step-by-step application of derivative formulas.
Q21.142%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the derivative formulas for inverse trigonometric functions. Re-teach the standard derivatives and their derivations.
Q2271%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the cross product concept and calculation methods. Re-teach vector cross product formula and determinant method.
Q2229%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know cross product method but make errors in setting up or executing the calculation steps. Practice step-by-step cross product problems.
Q2383%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to work with algebra of inverse trigonometric functions. Re-teach the fundamental properties and identities.
Q2317%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the approach but make errors in algebraic manipulation of inverse trig expressions. Practice step-by-step procedures.
Q2450%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand continuity conditions at a point. Re-teach left-hand limit, right-hand limit, and function value equality.
Q2440%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the continuity method but make errors in limit calculations. Practice more limit evaluation techniques.
Q2410%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in simplification. Focus on algebraic manipulation practice.
Q25.150%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply integration by substitution. Re-teach the method of identifying appropriate substitutions and transforming integrals.
Q25.150%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the substitution method but make errors in the procedural steps. Practice more examples focusing on correct application of the chain rule in reverse.
Q2686%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand conditional probability concepts and formulas. Re-teach P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B) with clear examples.
Q2614%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the probability scenario or confused the given information. Practice reading conditional probability word problems carefully.
Q2788%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to set up rate of change problems. Re-teach the relationship between variables and their derivatives.
Q2712%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying chain rule or differentiation steps. Practice step-by-step differentiation.
Q28.156%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for differentiating exponential and logarithmic functions but make execution errors in applying the rules. Practice more differentiation problems with step-by-step verification.
Q28.144%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental rules for differentiating exponential and logarithmic functions. Re-teach the basic differentiation formulas and their applications.
Q29.1100%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for finding equation of a line but make mistakes in executing the steps. Review the step-by-step procedure.
Q3075%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know how to set up area calculations but make mistakes in the integration process or evaluation steps. Review integration techniques and bounds evaluation.
Q3025%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up area calculations using integrals. Re-teach the fundamental concept of area under curves.
Q3146%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the graphical method for linear programming problems. Re-teach how to plot constraints and identify the feasible region.
Q3146%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in plotting lines or finding corner points. Practice step-by-step constraint plotting.
Q318%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what the linear programming problem is asking for. Emphasize careful reading of objective function and constraints.
Q32100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to find the angle between lines in 3D space. Re-teach the formula for angle between direction vectors and the concept of direction ratios.
Q3359%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the adjoint and inverse method but make errors in step execution. Practice the standard algorithm systematically.
Q3341%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship between adjoint and inverse of matrices. Re-teach the fundamental concepts step-by-step.
Q34.1100%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up and solve differential equations for growth/decay applications. Re-teach the fundamental approach to modeling exponential growth and decay problems.
Q35.156%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the integration rules but make execution errors in applying them. Drill step-by-step practice with careful attention to each step.
Q35.144%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental rules of integration. Re-teach the basic integration rules and their applications.
Q3650%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply Bayes' Theorem or conditional probability formulas. Re-teach the fundamental concepts with step-by-step examples.
Q3650%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the concepts but make arithmetic errors in probability calculations. Practice computational skills with fractions and decimals.
Q37100%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the maxima and minima method but make execution errors in the steps. Review common procedural mistakes and drill the step-by-step process.
Q38100%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental concepts of types of functions and types of relations. Re-teach the classification criteria for both functions and relations with clear examples.

46%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the formula and method but make arithmetic errors in cross products, dot products, or final calculations. Practice vector calculations step-by-step.I · Q29.2
36%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the formula for shortest distance between two skew lines. Re-teach the formula: d = |(a₂-a₁)·(b₁×b₂)|/|b₁×b₂|.I · Q29.2
18%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula but mix up direction vectors or position vectors. Emphasize careful identification of a₁, a₂, b₁, b₂ from line equations.I · Q29.2

52%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in setting up limits or forgetting the factor of 2. Drill the steps for absolute value integration.I · Q30
26%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value in integration or misinterpret the curve equation. Re-teach absolute value graphing and integration setup.I · Q30
13%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up correctly but make arithmetic errors in integration or limits evaluation. Practice integration calculations.I · Q30
9%Read Questions WrongStudents solve different problems or misread the curve equation. Emphasize careful reading of integration problems.I · Q30

62%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the linear programming method but make errors in finding corner points or evaluating the objective function. Review systematic approach to corner point identification.I · Q31
15%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up feasible regions or handle unbounded regions. Re-teach graphical method fundamentals.I · Q31
15%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret constraints or objective function setup. Emphasize careful reading of linear programming problems.I · Q31
8%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in computing objective function values. Practice numerical calculations.I · Q31

52%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to find lines through origin at fixed angles to given lines. Re-teach the formula for angle between lines using direction ratios.I · Q32
48%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the angle formula but make errors in setting up equations or solving the quadratic. Practice step-by-step problem solving.I · Q32
Section I · Q33 | Determinants✔ PASS
60%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ but make errors in cofactor calculations or matrix multiplication steps. Drill the systematic calculation of cofactors and matrix multiplication.I · Q33
20%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the inverse formula (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ or matrix inversion methods. Re-teach the property of inverse of product and adjugate method.I · Q33
15%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in determinant calculation or matrix multiplication. Practice computational accuracy with sign errors.I · Q33
5%Read Questions WrongStudents solve for B instead of (AB)⁻¹ or compute AB first then find inverse. Emphasize using the direct property (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹.I · Q33
Section I · Q34.1 | Differential Equations✔ PASS
50%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up differential equations for growth/decay problems. Re-teach the fundamental concept that rate of change is proportional to current quantity.I · Q34.1
50%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the differential equation setup right but make arithmetic errors when calculating the constant or final time. Practice logarithm calculations and substitution steps.I · Q34.1
Section I · Q34.2 | Differential Equations✔ PASS
43%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the differential equation structure and make errors in applying the product rule. Clarify the correct form of linear differential equations.I · Q34.2
43%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the method for solving linear differential equations. Re-teach the integrating factor technique step-by-step.I · Q34.2
14%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in the integration steps. Practice integration by parts with polynomial and trigonometric functions.I · Q34.2
Section I · Q35.1 | Integrals✔ PASS
52%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the partial fraction method but make errors in factorization, coefficient calculation, or integration steps. Practice systematic coefficient substitution and verification.I · Q35.1
48%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand partial fraction decomposition for rational functions. Re-teach the systematic approach for proper factorization and coefficient determination.I · Q35.1
Section I · Q35.2 | Integrals✔ PASS
92%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply properties of definite integrals like King's property for this type of problem. Re-teach the standard approach of using ∫₀ᵃ f(x)dx = ∫₀ᵃ f(a-x)dx.I · Q35.2
8%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the property and method but make errors in limit evaluation or substitution steps. Practice the complete solution methodology.I · Q35.2
Section I · Q36 | Probability✔ PASS
54%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the proper application of total probability theorem and Bayes' theorem in conditional probability problems. Re-teach these fundamental probability concepts with step-by-step examples.I · Q36
21%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what probability is being asked for in each part, especially confusing conditional probability with joint probability. Clarify the difference between P(A|B) and P(A∩B).I · Q36
13%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the probability formulas but make errors in applying Bayes' theorem correctly, especially in the denominator calculation. Practice step-by-step Bayes' theorem problems.I · Q36
12%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors when multiplying fractions and finding common denominators. Practice fraction calculations and decimal conversions.I · Q36
Section I · Q37 | Application Of Derivatives✔ PASS
54%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to set up the area function for a rectangle inscribed in an ellipse. Re-teach the method of expressing area as A = 4xy using ellipse equation to substitute for y.I · Q37
23%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in differentiating the area function or applying the product rule. Drill the differentiation steps systematically.I · Q37
15%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method and steps right but mess up arithmetic in solving a² - 2x² = 0. Practice algebraic manipulation and simplification.I · Q37
8%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the problem as finding area using integration instead of optimization. Clarify that this is a maximization problem using derivatives.I · Q37
Section I · Q38 | Relations & Functions✔ PASS
67%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of injective vs surjective functions or the properties of equivalence relations. Re-teach the fundamental definitions and methods for checking these properties.I · Q38
33%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the approach but make errors in the logical flow or skip crucial steps in proving injectivity/surjectivity. Practice step-by-step proof writing.I · Q38

68%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the derivative formula for cos⁻¹(x) or how to apply chain rule with composite functions. Re-teach the derivative formulas for inverse trigonometric functions step-by-step.J · Q21.1
32%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the derivative formula but make errors in applying quotient rule or simplifying complex expressions. Practice chain rule with quotient rule systematically.J · Q21.1
Section J · Q21.2 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
63%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the parametric differentiation method but make errors in applying the chain rule or computing d²y/dx². Practice the formula d²y/dx² = (d/dθ(dy/dx))/(dx/dθ).J · Q21.2
21%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of second-order parametric differentiation. Re-teach the relationship between parametric equations and second derivatives.J · Q21.2
11%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the parametric equations or confuse the required derivative. Emphasize careful reading of parametric forms and what derivative is being asked.J · Q21.2

Section J · Q22 | Vector Algebra✔ PASS

26%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in expanding the cross product or substituting given conditions. Practice step-by-step expansion of (a⃗ - d⃗) × (b⃗ - c⃗).J · Q22
4%Read Questions WrongStudents confused dot product with cross product for parallel vectors. Clarify that parallel vectors have cross product = 0, not dot product = 0.J · Q22

20%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but mess up arithmetic in final calculations. Practice computational accuracy.J · Q24

15%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in integration or evaluation. Practice computational accuracy.J · Q25.1
