29%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the basic relationship but make errors in differentiation or algebraic manipulation. Practice step-by-step differentiation of related rate problems.N · Q27
Section N · Q28.1 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
64%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply logarithmic differentiation to implicit functions. Re-teach the method of taking logarithms of both sides before differentiating.N · Q28.1
36%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to take logarithms but make errors in the differentiation steps or algebraic manipulation. Practice step-by-step logarithmic differentiation.N · Q28.1
Section N · Q28.2 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
56%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know implicit differentiation method but make errors in algebraic manipulation when isolating dy/dx. Practice rearranging complex expressions with multiple dy/dx terms.N · Q28.2
44%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand implicit differentiation for trigonometric functions or product rule application. Re-teach chain rule and product rule for composite trig functions.N · Q28.2
Section N · Q29.1 | 3d Geometry✔ PASS
50%Read Questions WrongStudents solve for shortest distance between skew lines instead of distance from point to line. Review the difference between these two concepts.N · Q29.1
50%Foundational GapsStudents lack understanding of 3D line equations and perpendicular distance formulas. Re-teach the fundamental concepts and methods.N · Q29.1
Section N · Q29.2 | 3d Geometry✔ PASS
43%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the correct formula for shortest distance between skew lines. Re-teach the vector formula for distance between two skew lines.N · Q29.2
43%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the method but make arithmetic errors in cross products and dot products. Practice vector calculations step-by-step.N · Q29.2
14%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula but apply it incorrectly or skip critical steps. Emphasize proper sequence of operations.N · Q29.2
Section N · Q30 | Application Of Integrals✔ PASS
67%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents understand the concept of absolute value and area calculation but make errors in setting up proper limits or integrating correctly. Review integration techniques and limit determination.N · Q30
22%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value equations or set up area integrals properly. Re-teach the relationship between |y| and piecewise functions.N · Q30
11%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret the equation x + 2|y| = 1 or the area calculation requirements. Emphasize careful reading of absolute value notation.N · Q30
Section N · Q31 | Linear Programming✔ PASS
72%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in finding corner points or evaluating the objective function. Practice intersection calculations and systematic evaluation.N · Q31
16%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the complete graphical method for linear programming. Re-teach feasible region identification and corner point evaluation.N · Q31
12%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in calculating intersection points or objective function values. Practice fraction arithmetic.N · Q31
Section N · Q32 | 3d Geometry✔ PASS
50%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to find angle between lines using dot product formula. Re-teach the concept of direction ratios and angle formula.N · Q32
44%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in setting up equations or algebraic manipulation. Practice the step-by-step procedure.N · Q32
6%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in calculations. Focus on careful computation practice.N · Q32
Section N · Q33 | Determinants✔ PASS
55%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formula (AB)⁻¹ = B⁻¹A⁻¹ but make errors in computing adjoint or matrix multiplication. Review step-by-step cofactor calculation and matrix multiplication rules.N · Q33
28%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in determinant calculation or matrix multiplication. Practice basic arithmetic operations with matrices.N · Q33
11%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship between adjoint, determinant and inverse matrices. Re-teach the fundamental concepts of matrix inverse.N · Q33
6%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the matrix elements or formula. Emphasize careful reading of given matrices and formulas.N · Q33
Section N · Q34.1 | Differential Equations✔ PASS
75%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up differential equations for growth/decay problems. Re-teach the exponential growth model dN/dt = kN and its applications.N · Q34.1
25%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the differential equation approach but make arithmetic errors in logarithmic calculations. Practice solving exponential equations with logarithms.N · Q34.1
Section N · Q34.2 | Differential Equations✔ PASS
80%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method of solving linear differential equations but make errors in rearranging terms or setting up the standard form. Drill the systematic approach to converting to dy/dx + Py = Q form.N · Q34.2
20%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the differential equation or substituted incorrect terms. Emphasize careful reading and verification of the given equation before proceeding.N · Q34.2
Section N · Q35.1 | Integrals✔ PASS
56%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to set up partial fraction decomposition for rational functions. Re-teach the method of breaking down complex fractions into simpler forms.N · Q35.1
44%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know partial fractions but make errors in coefficient calculation or algebraic manipulation. Practice systematic approaches to finding A, B, C values.N · Q35.1
Section N · Q35.2 | Integrals✔ PASS
80%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply King's property for definite integrals with trigonometric expressions. Re-teach the property and practice with similar problems.N · Q35.2
20%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying trigonometric identities or substitution. Drill the step-by-step procedure.N · Q35.2
Section N · Q36 | Probability✔ PASS
44%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the law of total probability and Bayes' theorem for conditional probability problems. Re-teach these fundamental concepts with step-by-step examples.N · Q36
22%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the formulas but apply them incorrectly or skip crucial steps in Bayes' theorem calculations. Drill the proper sequence of steps.N · Q36
22%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method but make arithmetic errors when multiplying probabilities and fractions. Practice computational accuracy.N · Q36
12%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what the question is asking for in conditional probability contexts. Emphasize careful reading of probability statements.N · Q36
Section N · Q37 | Application Of Derivatives✔ PASS
63%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to set up area functions for rectangles inscribed in ellipses. Re-teach the method of expressing area in terms of one variable using ellipse equation.N · Q37
25%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in differentiation or critical point calculation. Drill the steps for finding derivatives of composite functions.N · Q37
12%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what to find in maxima-minima problems. Clarify that they need to find maximum area, not solve general ellipse properties.N · Q37
Section N · Q38 | Relations & Functions✔ PASS
50%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of injectivity and surjectivity, or how to prove equivalence relations. Re-teach function properties and relation types step-by-step.N · Q38
33%Read Questions WrongStudents misread parts of the question or focused on wrong aspects. Emphasize careful reading and understanding what needs to be proven.N · Q38
17%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in algebraic manipulation or logical steps. Practice systematic proof techniques.N · Q38
Section O · Q21.1 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
80%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to differentiate inverse trigonometric functions using chain rule. Re-teach the derivatives of inverse trig functions and chain rule application.O · Q21.1
13%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying chain rule or simplifying expressions. Practice step-by-step differentiation with feedback.O · Q21.1
7%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the function form or variable. Emphasize careful reading of the given function before starting differentiation.O · Q21.1
Section O · Q21.2 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
67%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the parametric differentiation method but make errors in applying chain rule for second derivative or incorrect algebraic manipulation. Drill the complete process step-by-step.O · Q21.2
27%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand parametric differentiation or confuse the given equations. Re-teach the concept of finding dy/dx and d²y/dx² for parametric equations.O · Q21.2
6%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in trigonometric calculations. Practice trigonometric computations.O · Q21.2
Section O · Q22 | Vector Algebra✔ PASS
84%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to use cross product properties to prove vector parallelism. Re-teach the method of expanding cross products and using distributive properties.O · Q22
11%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the correct approach but make errors in manipulating the cross product expressions. Practice step-by-step algebraic manipulation of vector equations.O · Q22
5%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make sign errors when applying cross product properties. Drill the anti-commutative property of cross products.O · Q22
Section O · Q23 | Inverse Trigonometric Functions✔ PASS
100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use the sin(A+B) expansion formula to simplify (sin x + cos x)/√2. Re-teach the technique of writing 1/√2 as sin(π/4) = cos(π/4).O · Q23
Section O · Q24 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
50%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand continuity conditions or how to set up LHL = RHL equations. Re-teach continuity definition and limit evaluation methods.O · Q24
42%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the continuity approach but make errors in limit calculations or algebraic manipulation. Drill rationalization techniques and limit evaluation steps.O · Q24
8%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in squaring or sign handling. Practice basic algebraic computations.O · Q24

65%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply integration by substitution method. Re-teach the substitution technique step-by-step.O · Q25.1
21%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the substitution method but make errors in transforming dx or manipulating the integrand. Practice substitution steps carefully.O · Q25.1
14%Read Questions WrongStudents solve a different integral or misread the function. Clarify what exactly needs to be integrated.O · Q25.1
Section O · Q25.2 | Application Of Integrals✔ PASS
62%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the integration method but make errors in handling absolute values or setting up limits incorrectly. Review when to split integrals and proper limit handling.O · Q25.2
23%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the integral correctly but make arithmetic errors in evaluation or substitution. Practice careful calculation steps.O · Q25.2
15%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of area under curves or integration setup. Re-teach basic integration for area calculation.O · Q25.2

53%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of 'at least two' events and how to calculate it using complementary cases. Re-teach the systematic approach for 'at least' problems.O · Q26

5%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method and steps right but make arithmetic errors in fraction calculations. Practice fraction multiplication and addition.O · Q26

29%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the relationship but make errors in differentiation steps or algebraic manipulation. Practice chain rule and related rates systematically.O · Q27
14%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but use wrong formula values like m = √3a/4 instead of √3a/2. Drill the exact formulas for equilateral triangle properties.O · Q27

40%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the concept of conditional probability and Bayes' theorem. Re-teach the fundamental formulas and when to apply them.O · Q36

17%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but skip crucial algebraic steps in proving injectivity. Practice step-by-step algebraic manipulation.O · Q38

Section P · Q35.2 | Integrals✔ PASS
64%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand properties of definite integrals for symmetric functions. Re-teach the property that ∫[0 to a] f(x)dx = ∫[0 to a] f(a-x)dx and how to use it to simplify integrals.P · Q35.2
27%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the approach but make errors in applying the substitution or symmetry property correctly. Practice step-by-step application of definite integral properties.P · Q35.2
9%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in the final calculation. Review computation of inverse trigonometric function limits and fraction simplification.P · Q35.2
Section P · Q36 | Probability✔ PASS
75%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply Bayes' theorem and conditional probability formulas correctly. Re-teach the fundamental concepts with step-by-step examples.P · Q36
19%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the concepts but make errors in setting up the probability formulas or miss key steps in the calculation process. Practice more structured problem-solving approaches.P · Q36
6%Make Calculation MistakesStudents understand the method but make arithmetic errors when computing fractions and percentages. Practice computational accuracy with probability calculations.P · Q36
Section P · Q37 | Application Of Derivatives✔ PASS
40%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up maxima-minima problems with constraints. Re-teach the method of expressing one variable in terms of another using the constraint.P · Q37
20%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the optimization method but make errors in setting up the area function or applying differentiation rules. Drill the step-by-step procedure for constrained optimization.P · Q37
20%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in differentiation or algebraic manipulation. Practice chain rule and product rule calculations.P · Q37
20%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand what needs to be optimized or how to interpret the geometric constraints. Clarify problem statement interpretation.P · Q37
Section P · Q38 | Relations & Functions✔ PASS
46%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the proper method to prove injectivity using the definition f(x₁) = f(x₂) implies x₁ = x₂. Re-teach the fundamental approach to proving one-to-one functions.P · Q38
31%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the injectivity test method but make errors in algebraic manipulation or skip crucial steps in the proof. Practice step-by-step algebraic simplification.P · Q38
15%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand what needs to be proven or confuse the domain/codomain requirements. Clarify the difference between proving injectivity and surjectivity.P · Q38
8%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in factoring or solving quadratic equations. Review basic algebraic operations.P · Q38
Section Q · Q21.1 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the correct derivative formula for inverse cosine or make fundamental errors in applying chain rule and quotient rule. Re-teach derivatives of inverse trigonometric functions systematically.Q · Q21.1
Section Q · Q21.2 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
50%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the correct formula for second derivative of parametric equations. Re-teach that d²y/dx² = (d/dθ)(dy/dx) ÷ (dx/dθ).Q · Q21.2
42%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying chain rule or substituting values at θ = π/6. Practice step-by-step differentiation.Q · Q21.2
8%Read Questions WrongStudents attempted a different approach or misunderstood the parametric form. Clarify the standard parametric differentiation method.Q · Q21.2
Section Q · Q22 | Vector Algebra✔ PASS
67%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to manipulate vector cross products to prove parallelism. Re-teach cross product properties and parallel vector conditions.Q · Q22
33%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know cross product properties but make errors in algebraic manipulation or logical flow. Practice step-by-step proof techniques.Q · Q22
Section Q · Q23 | Inverse Trigonometric Functions✔ PASS
83%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to use trigonometric identities to simplify expressions inside inverse trig functions. Re-teach the identity sin x + cos x = √2 sin(x + π/4).Q · Q23
17%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents recognize the identity but make errors in applying it correctly. Drill the step-by-step process of using sin(A + B) = sin A cos B + cos A sin B.Q · Q23
Section Q · Q24 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
50%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to check LHL = RHL for continuity but make errors in limit evaluation or algebraic manipulation. Review limit calculation techniques and rationalization methods.Q · Q24
50%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the condition for continuity of piecewise functions or confuse endpoint evaluation with limit evaluation. Re-teach continuity conditions for piecewise functions.Q · Q24
Section Q · Q25.1 | Integrals✔ PASS
100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply integration by substitution correctly for complex logarithmic expressions. Re-teach the substitution method step-by-step with similar problems.Q · Q25.1
Section Q · Q25.2 | Application Of Integrals✔ PASS
40%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand that area below x-axis needs absolute value or splitting at x-intercept. Re-teach concept of signed vs unsigned area.Q · Q25.2
40%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to split integral but make errors in setting up limits or forget absolute value bars. Drill the step-by-step process.Q · Q25.2
20%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get method right but make arithmetic errors in evaluating definite integrals. Practice limit substitution calculations.Q · Q25.2
Section Q · Q26 | Probability✔ PASS
87%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand that 'at least two' means exactly two OR all three hitting. They confuse independent events with conditional probability and use wrong formulas.Q · Q26
13%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the correct approach for 'at least two' but make errors in setting up or combining the three cases properly.Q · Q26
Section Q · Q27 | Application Of Derivatives✔ PASS
60%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the relationship between median and side of equilateral triangle but make errors in differentiation or algebraic manipulation. Drill the differentiation steps and emphasize careful algebraic work.Q · Q27
30%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the geometric relationship between median and side in an equilateral triangle or confuse this problem with unrelated concepts. Re-teach the formula for median of equilateral triangle and its derivation.Q · Q27
10%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in the final calculation. Practice computational skills with square roots and fractions.Q · Q27
Section Q · Q28.1 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
64%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the logarithmic differentiation method for implicit differentiation. Re-teach taking log of both sides and applying differentiation rules.Q · Q28.1
36%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in algebraic manipulation or product rule application. Drill the step-by-step process of logarithmic differentiation.Q · Q28.1
Section Q · Q28.2 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
60%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply implicit differentiation to trigonometric equations. Re-teach the chain rule and product rule for implicit differentiation step-by-step.Q · Q28.2
40%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differentiation rules but make errors in algebraic manipulation or substitution steps. Practice more problems involving trigonometric identities and algebraic simplification.Q · Q28.2
Section Q · Q29.1 | 3d Geometry✔ PASS
57%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the cross product and formula setup right but make arithmetic errors in final calculations. Practice computational accuracy with vector operations.Q · Q29.1
29%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the shortest distance formula but make errors in cross product calculations or component operations. Drill the step-by-step procedure.Q · Q29.1
14%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the formula for shortest distance between skew lines. Re-teach the concept and formula derivation.Q · Q29.1
Section Q · Q29.2 | 3d Geometry✔ PASS
57%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the cross product and formula setup right but make arithmetic errors in final calculations. Practice computational accuracy with vector operations.Q · Q29.2
29%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the shortest distance formula but make errors in cross product calculations or component operations. Drill the step-by-step procedure.Q · Q29.2
14%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the formula for shortest distance between skew lines. Re-teach the concept and formula derivation.Q · Q29.2
Section Q · Q30 | Application Of Integrals✔ PASS
77%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to handle absolute value functions in area problems. Re-teach how |y| creates symmetric regions and requires splitting the integral.Q · Q30
15%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the setup but make errors in integral limits or miss the symmetry factor. Practice identifying correct bounds for absolute value curves.Q · Q30
8%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up the integral correctly but make arithmetic errors in evaluation. Practice integration calculations with fractions.Q · Q30
Section Q · Q31 | Linear Programming✔ PASS
53%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in finding corner points or evaluating objective function values. Review intersection calculations and function evaluation steps.Q · Q31
40%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand when linear programming problems have no minimum or don't recognize unbounded feasible regions. Re-teach the concept of bounded vs unbounded regions.Q · Q31
7%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in calculating objective function values. Practice computation of negative fractions.Q · Q31
Section Q · Q32 | 3d Geometry✔ PASS
92%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the formula for angle between lines. They confuse direction vectors, incorrectly apply the dot product formula, or use wrong coordinates. Re-teach the concept: cos θ = |b₁·b₂|/(|b₁||b₂|).Q · Q32
8%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but fail to complete the solution properly. They start correctly but don't follow through with all required steps to find the equation of lines.Q · Q32
Section Q · Q33 | Determinants✔ PASS
47%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in matrix multiplication or cofactor calculations. Drill the step-by-step process for finding inverse matrices.Q · Q33
27%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in cofactors or matrix operations. Practice computational accuracy.Q · Q33
26%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the property (AB)^-1 = B^-1 A^-1 or confuse the order. Re-teach inverse properties of matrix multiplication.Q · Q33
Section Q · Q34.1 | Differential Equations✔ PASS
50%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the linear differential equation method but make errors in applying product rule or setting up the standard form. Review step-by-step procedure for solving dy/dx + Py = Q.Q · Q34.1
38%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in integration by parts or computing the integrating factor. Practice integration techniques for x²sinx and x²logx.Q · Q34.1
12%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the differential equation structure and apply wrong coefficient for P in the standard form. Emphasize careful identification of coefficients in dy/dx + Py = Q.Q · Q34.1
Section Q · Q34.2 | Differential Equations✔ PASS
50%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the linear differential equation method but make errors in applying product rule or setting up the standard form. Review step-by-step procedure for solving dy/dx + Py = Q.Q · Q34.2
38%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in integration by parts or computing the integrating factor. Practice integration techniques for x²sinx and x²logx.Q · Q34.2
12%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the differential equation structure and apply wrong coefficient for P in the standard form. Emphasize careful identification of coefficients in dy/dx + Py = Q.Q · Q34.2
Section Q · Q35.1 | Integrals✔ PASS
70%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to factorize the denominator or set up partial fractions for rational function integration. Re-teach factorization techniques and partial fraction decomposition methods step-by-step.Q · Q35.1
30%Make Calculation MistakesStudents set up partial fractions correctly but make arithmetic errors when solving for constants or integrating individual terms. Practice coefficient comparison and basic integration formulas.Q · Q35.1
Section Q · Q35.2 | Integrals✔ PASS
75%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply properties of definite integrals to evaluate this integral. Re-teach the property ∫₀ᵃ f(x)dx = ∫₀ᵃ f(a-x)dx and its application.Q · Q35.2
25%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstood what the question was asking for. Review the question requirements and emphasize careful reading of the problem statement.Q · Q35.2
Section Q · Q36 | Probability✔ PASS
56%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to apply total probability law or Bayes' theorem properly. They misinterpret given probabilities and add them directly without considering conditional relationships. Re-teach conditional probability and the multiplication rule.Q · Q36
31%Read Questions WrongStudents confuse production percentages with defect rates and mix up conditional probability notation. They read P(D|A) as P(A|D) or interpret machine output rates as defect probabilities. Clarify probability notation and problem setup.Q · Q36
13%Make Calculation MistakesStudents know the correct formulas but make arithmetic errors when multiplying fractions or converting percentages. Practice fraction multiplication and percentage calculations.Q · Q36
Section Q · Q37 | Application Of Derivatives✔ PASS
60%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know to find area as length × breadth and differentiate, but make algebraic errors in solving for critical points. Practice solving the derivative equation systematically.Q · Q37
20%Read Questions WrongStudents misunderstand the problem setup and don't use the ellipse constraint properly. Clarify how to inscribe a rectangle in an ellipse.Q · Q37
20%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand optimization problems and confuse area with perimeter. Re-teach the maxima-minima method for constrained optimization.Q · Q37
Section Q · Q38 | Relations & Functions✔ PASS
50%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for proving injectivity but make logical errors in algebraic steps. Review step-by-step proof techniques for one-one functions.Q · Q38
25%Read Questions WrongStudents attempt wrong parts or misunderstand the question requirements. Clarify what needs to be proven for each subpart.Q · Q38
17%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the definitions of injective and surjective functions. Re-teach fundamental concepts of function types.Q · Q38
8%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in algebraic manipulation. Practice algebraic simplification.Q · Q38
Section R · Q21.1 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
58%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the differentiation rules for inverse trigonometric functions but make errors in applying the chain rule or simplification steps. Drill the step-by-step application of derivative formulas.R · Q21.1
42%Foundational GapsStudents don't know the derivative formulas for inverse trigonometric functions. Re-teach the standard derivatives and their derivations.R · Q21.1
Section R · Q22 | Vector Algebra✔ PASS
71%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the cross product concept and calculation methods. Re-teach vector cross product formula and determinant method.R · Q22
29%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know cross product method but make errors in setting up or executing the calculation steps. Practice step-by-step cross product problems.R · Q22
Section R · Q23 | Inverse Trigonometric Functions✔ PASS
83%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to work with algebra of inverse trigonometric functions. Re-teach the fundamental properties and identities.R · Q23
17%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the approach but make errors in algebraic manipulation of inverse trig expressions. Practice step-by-step procedures.R · Q23
Section R · Q24 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
50%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand continuity conditions at a point. Re-teach left-hand limit, right-hand limit, and function value equality.R · Q24
40%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the continuity method but make errors in limit calculations. Practice more limit evaluation techniques.R · Q24
10%Make Calculation MistakesStudents get the method right but make arithmetic errors in simplification. Focus on algebraic manipulation practice.R · Q24
Section R · Q25.1 | Integrals✔ PASS
50%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to apply integration by substitution. Re-teach the method of identifying appropriate substitutions and transforming integrals.R · Q25.1
50%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the substitution method but make errors in the procedural steps. Practice more examples focusing on correct application of the chain rule in reverse.R · Q25.1
Section R · Q26 | Probability✔ PASS
86%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand conditional probability concepts and formulas. Re-teach P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B) with clear examples.R · Q26
14%Read Questions WrongStudents misread the probability scenario or confused the given information. Practice reading conditional probability word problems carefully.R · Q26
Section R · Q27 | Application Of Derivatives✔ PASS
88%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to set up rate of change problems. Re-teach the relationship between variables and their derivatives.R · Q27
12%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method but make errors in applying chain rule or differentiation steps. Practice step-by-step differentiation.R · Q27
Section R · Q28.1 | Continuity & Differentiability✔ PASS
56%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for differentiating exponential and logarithmic functions but make execution errors in applying the rules. Practice more differentiation problems with step-by-step verification.R · Q28.1
44%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental rules for differentiating exponential and logarithmic functions. Re-teach the basic differentiation formulas and their applications.R · Q28.1
Section R · Q29.1 | 3d Geometry✔ PASS
100%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the method for finding equation of a line but make mistakes in executing the steps. Review the step-by-step procedure.R · Q29.1
Section R · Q30 | Application Of Integrals✔ PASS
75%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know how to set up area calculations but make mistakes in the integration process or evaluation steps. Review integration techniques and bounds evaluation.R · Q30
25%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand how to set up area calculations using integrals. Re-teach the fundamental concept of area under curves.R · Q30
Section R · Q31 | Linear Programming✔ PASS
46%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the graphical method for linear programming problems. Re-teach how to plot constraints and identify the feasible region.R · Q31
46%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the graphical method but make errors in plotting lines or finding corner points. Practice step-by-step constraint plotting.R · Q31
8%Read Questions WrongStudents misinterpret what the linear programming problem is asking for. Emphasize careful reading of objective function and constraints.R · Q31
Section R · Q32 | 3d Geometry✔ PASS
100%Foundational GapsStudents don't know how to find the angle between lines in 3D space. Re-teach the formula for angle between direction vectors and the concept of direction ratios.R · Q32

59%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the adjoint and inverse method but make errors in step execution. Practice the standard algorithm systematically.R · Q33
41%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the relationship between adjoint and inverse of matrices. Re-teach the fundamental concepts step-by-step.R · Q33
Section R · Q34.1 | Differential Equations✔ PASS

56%Make Mistakes in StepsStudents know the integration rules but make execution errors in applying them. Drill step-by-step practice with careful attention to each step.R · Q35.1
44%Foundational GapsStudents don't understand the fundamental rules of integration. Re-teach the basic integration rules and their applications.R · Q35.1
